papers.title: Hermes Agent: Self-Improving AI Agent with Closed Learning Loop
papers.core_contribution: 开源的自我进化 AI Agent 框架，具备闭环学习能力（技能自动创建与改进、持久记忆、用户建模），支持 20+ 平台网关和 6 种终端后端，从 $5 VPS 到 GPU 集群均可运行。
papers.summary:
Hermes Agent 是 Nous Research 构建的开源 AI Agent，核心特点是「自我改进闭环」：Agent 在完成复杂任务后自动创建技能（Skill），后续使用中持续改进技能，并通过持久记忆和用户画像实现跨会话的个性化。

架构上，项目以 run_agent.py（10K 行）中的 AIAgent.run_conversation 为核心循环，实现了多 Provider 适配（OpenAI/Anthropic/OpenRouter/200+ 模型）、流式响应、工具调用（40+ 内置工具）、上下文压缩、会话持久化（JSONL + SQLite FTS5）、子代理委托等。Gateway 系统支持 Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/飞书/钉钉等 20+ 平台，统一通过 BasePlatformAdapter 抽象层适配。

项目还包含面向研究的批量轨迹生成（batch_runner.py）、轨迹压缩（trajectory_compressor.py）和 Atropos RL 环境集成，用于训练下一代工具调用模型。v0.8.0 版本已实现 MCP 双向集成（既是 MCP 客户端也是 MCP 服务端）、ACP 适配器、cron 调度等企业级功能。
key_findings: 核心循环 AIAgent.run_conversation 采用「双轨消息」设计——messages（持久化历史）与 api_messages（每轮构造的请求视图）分离，实现 prefix cache 稳定性与 ephemeral 注入的解耦
工具系统分两条路径：registry.dispatch（40+ 普通工具）和 _invoke_tool（todo/memory/session_search/delegate 等需要 Agent 状态的工具），避免了循环依赖
技能系统实现了完整的自我改进闭环：创建→使用→发现问题→自动 patch→改进，技能存储为 YAML frontmatter + Markdown，兼容 agentskills.io 开放标准
上下文压缩采用两层架构：运行时 ContextCompressor（50% 阈值触发 LLM 摘要）+ 网关卫生压缩（85% 阈值预压缩），压缩后 SQLite 会话自动分裂（parent_session_id 链）
Gateway 通过工厂模式 + BasePlatformAdapter 抽象支持 20+ 平台，所有平台共享统一的授权/命令/agent 管道
错误恢复设计极为完善：classify_api_error 统一分类→credential pool 轮换→fallback provider→jittered backoff→stale stream 检测→可中断重试
limitations: run_agent.py 单文件 10K+ 行，传输层、业务编排、边界情况恢复耦合在一起，可维护性是挑战
工具执行存在两条并行路径（registry vs agent 内联），逻辑有重复，顺序路径中 todo/memory 等工具的处理是内联 copy-paste
缺乏类型注解和接口定义，核心数据结构用 Dict 传递而非 dataclass/Pydantic model
无 GPU 相关功能（纯 CPU Agent），不直接涉及推理加速或训练计算
测试覆盖率不明确，部分核心路径（如压缩后会话分裂）的测试可能不充分

infra_impact.algorithm: 提供 Atropos RL 环境和轨迹生成管线，可用于训练下一代工具调用模型
infra_impact.kernel: N/A
infra_impact.framework: N/A
infra_impact.llm: 通过 200+ 模型支持和 smart routing，展示了模型无关 Agent 架构的可行性
infra_impact.agent: 定义了 Agent 自我改进闭环的参考实现：技能自动创建、使用中改进、持久记忆、跨会话用户建模
infra_impact.code: 项目本身是 Agent 类开源项目的标杆，其技能系统和 MCP 双向集成模式值得其他 Agent 框架参考

open_questions: run_agent.py 的 10K 行单文件结构是否可通过 state machine 或 plugin 架构拆分？
技能自我改进在实际使用中的成功率和改进质量如何量化评估？
上下文压缩的摘要质量如何影响多轮对话的一致性？
MCP 服务端模式下与 Cursor/Claude Code 的集成实际体验如何？

papers.title: OpenAI Agents SDK: Lightweight Multi-Agent Workflow Framework
papers.core_contribution: 轻量但功能强大的多Agent工作流编排框架，声明式定义Agent的指令/工具/护栏/协作关系，支持中断恢复、沙箱执行和实时语音
papers.summary:
OpenAI Agents SDK 是 OpenAI 官方的多 Agent 编排框架 (v0.14.1, 87K LOC)。核心概念包括 Agent（配置了指令、工具、护栏的 LLM）、Runner（无状态执行引擎）、Tool（函数/MCP/hosted 工具）、Handoff（Agent 间委托）、Guardrail（输入输出安全检查）、Session（会话持久化）、Tracing（全链路追踪）、Sandbox（隔离执行环境）和 Realtime/Voice（实时语音）。

Runner 通过 turn loop 编排执行：每轮调用模型 → 解析输出 → 分支为 FinalOutput/Handoff/RunAgain/Interruption。RunState 可序列化实现中断恢复(human-in-the-loop)。工具执行通过 asyncio.gather() 并行化。

项目 Provider-Agnostic 但 OpenAI-First：内部统一为 Responses API 格式，通过 LiteLLM/any-llm 支持 100+ 模型。MCP 作为一等公民原生集成。v0.14 新增 Sandbox Agent 支持 Docker/E2B/Modal 等 7 种后端。
key_findings: Runner turn loop 通过 NextStep 四分支(FinalOutput/Handoff/RunAgain/Interruption)实现灵活的执行流控制
RunState 完全可序列化为 JSON，实现生产级 human-in-the-loop 中断恢复
Agent-as-Tool 模式优雅地实现了 Agent 嵌套调用，与 Handoff 互补
Guardrails 与模型调用并行执行，零额外延迟的安全检查
Sandbox Capabilities 插件化架构，通过 Shell/Filesystem/Patch 能力组合支持长任务
Tracing 内建为一等需求而非插件，保证全链路可观测性
limitations: run_state.py 3304 行状态序列化逻辑过于集中，包含复杂 schema version 迁移
OpenAI Responses 格式作为内部数据格式与 OpenAI 绑定过深，其他 provider 需来回转换
Extensions 目录膨胀（7种sandbox/2种LLM/5种session provider），维护成本增长
Runner turn loop 扩展性有限，自定义底层调度逻辑不太容易
tool.py 1938 行 Tool Union 类型定义和 FunctionTool 实现混在一起

infra_impact.algorithm: N/A
infra_impact.kernel: N/A
infra_impact.framework: N/A
infra_impact.llm: 通过 LiteLLM/any-llm 支持 100+ 模型，展示了模型无关 Agent 架构
infra_impact.agent: 定义了多 Agent 编排的参考实现：Handoff + Agent-as-Tool + Guardrails + HITL
infra_impact.code: 21K stars 的官方 Agent SDK，其架构模式(RunState 可序列化、Tracing 内建、Sandbox 插件化)值得其他框架参考

open_questions: RunState schema version 迁移的长期可维护性如何保证？
OpenAI Responses 格式作为内部表示是否应该抽象为独立的中间格式？
Sandbox Agent 的 7 种后端是否应该拆分为独立包？

papers.title: TokenDance: Scaling Multi-Agent LLM Serving via Collective KV Cache Sharing
papers.core_contribution: Elevate KV cache reuse from per-request to per-round All-Gather collective, amortizing RoPE + important-position selection across N agents in one pass, and compressing sibling caches via Master + block-sparse diff storage for 11-17x compression and 2.7x more concurrent agents under SLO.
papers.summary: TokenDance targets the memory bottleneck of multi-agent LLM serving. Frameworks like GenerativeAgents and AgentSociety organize execution in synchronized rounds where each agent's prompt concatenates a private history with the same set of shared output blocks from the previous round, but at different absolute positions. Prefix caching fails because private histories differ in length, while position-independent caching (e.g., CacheBlend) performs redundant RoPE and diff analysis for every agent. TokenDance introduces three components: a round-aware prompt interface that preserves block boundaries via separator tokens; collective KV cache reuse that groups compatible requests and performs one shared RoPE + diff pass per round; and diff-aware Master-Mirror storage with fused restore that encodes sibling caches as block-sparse diffs merged inline with the transfer pipeline. On A100-80G with Qwen2.5-7B/14B evaluated on GenerativeAgents and AgentSociety, TokenDance supports up to 2.7x more concurrent agents under a 1500 ms SLO than vLLM with prefix caching, compresses per-agent KV storage by 11.2x (7B) to 17.5x (14B), achieves 2.57x peak prefill speedup over serial CacheBlend, and 1.3-2.6x fused restore speedup over dense reconstruction, while producing numerically identical output to the underlying CacheBlend PIC backend.
key_findings: The All-Gather pattern is a systemic opportunity: in 8-agent GenerativeAgents rounds, pairwise block-level KV similarity is 91-97%, yet vLLM treats each cache as independent, wasting most GPU memory.
Per-request PIC is structurally wasteful: N agents trigger N identical RoPE rotations and diff analyses; collective grouping amortizes these from O(N) to O(1).
Multi-agent memory grows as O(N * R); reducing prefill compute alone cannot solve scaling, per-agent storage must shrink.
Recovered KV caches differ in only 10-20% of block positions (avg 53.2 blocks of 500-700 for 7B, 59.6 for 14B), enabling 11.2-17.5x compression with Master + block-sparse diff.
Fused diff restore (ping-pong buffer + inline sparse merge in the transfer pipeline) is essential to preserve the compression benefit online, giving 1.3-2.6x speedup over dense reconstruction.

TokenDance's accuracy equals the underlying PIC backend (CacheBlend); 3 of 8 tested scenarios match vLLM bit-exactly under greedy decoding, the rest diverge 3.3-11.9% solely due to CacheBlend's selective recomputation.
Scaling gain grows with both agent count and model size: 14B benefits more because per-agent KV footprint doubles while the absolute number of differing blocks stays flat.
limitations: Strong dependence on the All-Gather pattern; DAG, tree, or partial-gather multi-agent topologies degrade compression and reuse.
Group compatibility requires same active prompt length, same cached span, and disjoint slot mappings; production schedulers may fail to form large groups and fall back to per-request PIC.
Accuracy is inherited from the PIC backend; applications that cannot tolerate CacheBlend-level drift must disable reuse and forfeit the speedup.
Fused restore currently operates in the transfer pipeline rather than inside the attention tile loader; deeper fusion is only suggested as future work.
Evaluation is limited to a single A100-80G with Qwen2.5-7B/14B; no data for H100/H200/MI300, MoE/MLA models, or multi-node TP/PP deployments.
No open-source repository cited in the paper as of April 2026; references to OpenClaw/MoltBook as evidence of real-world All-Gather adoption may be premature.

infra_impact.algorithm: Indirectly enables cheaper multi-agent RL rollouts where rollout workloads exhibit All-Gather structure; no direct training-algorithm change.
infra_impact.kernel: Introduces a paired block-sparse KV diff CUDA kernel (~500 lines) aligned with attention tile boundaries; presents an integration opportunity with FlashAttention/FlashInfer tile loaders.
infra_impact.framework: Primary contribution layer: establishes 'round' as a first-class scheduling and caching unit, likely to influence vLLM/SGLang toward round-aware APIs and diff-aware KV backends on top of LMCache.
infra_impact.llm: Architecture-agnostic in principle but currently bound to RoPE and vanilla K/V layout; adaptation to MLA (DeepSeek-V3), GQA, and MoE requires non-trivial redesign and is not evaluated.
infra_impact.agent: Directly benefits multi-agent runtimes (AgentSociety, GenerativeAgents, OpenClaw, MoltBook) by providing a standard round-aware prompt interface activated by a single separator-token change.
infra_impact.code: ~3K lines Python + 500 lines CUDA/C++ on top of LMCache + vLLM V1; no public repo referenced in the paper, but would be a major LMCache contribution if open-sourced.

open_questions: How does the Master-Mirror layout degrade quantitatively when the prompt structure deviates from pure All-Gather (DAG, partial gather, tree aggregation)? Is there a structural-similarity threshold that predicts the compression cliff?
Is the deviation-score-minimizing Master selection heuristic near-optimal, or are there pathological $\mathcal{O}^t$ distributions where it produces significantly larger diffs than an optimal choice?

How do the gains compound with per-cache quantization (KIVI, KVQuant) or eviction (H2O), and does quantizing the diff values interact destructively with CacheBlend's already-noisy selective recomputation?
How much does Master-Mirror need to be redesigned for MLA (latent KV) and MoE (per-token expert routing) architectures, and how large are the resulting Mirror diffs?
What is the achievable gain of pushing fused restore inside the FlashAttention tile loader (HBM -> SMEM) versus the current transfer-pipeline-level fusion?

papers.title: From Agent Loops to Structured Graphs: A Scheduler-Theoretic Framework for LLM Agent Execution
papers.core_contribution: Reframes LLM agent execution as a scheduler problem parameterized by ready-set cardinality |U| and policy explicitness, then proposes SGH (Structured Graph Harness)—a static-DAG runtime with plan-version immutability, three-layer separation, and a three-level escalation recovery protocol—to trade expressiveness for controllability and auditability.
papers.summary: The paper identifies three structural weaknesses of the dominant Agent Loop paradigm: implicit step dependencies, unbounded recovery loops, and mutable execution history. Using a formal scheduler tuple (S,U,P,O,Delta), it characterizes Agent Loop as a single-ready-unit scheduler (|U|<=1) with a non-deterministic LLM-driven policy, and places all agent execution systems (planner loops, LangGraph, classical workflow engines, SGH) on one continuum. The proposed Structured Graph Harness lifts control flow from context into an explicit static DAG, makes plans immutable within a version, separates planner/runtime/recovery into three layers, and enforces a strict recovery escalation (local_retry -> local_patch -> request_replan) at the API boundary. The paper formalizes a 10-state node state machine with bounded termination and conditional soundness proofs (with an explicit validation-gap bound Pr[correct] >= prod p_v), supports all_of/any_of join semantics while excluding first_of competitive parallelism, and designs a seven-group experimental protocol (G0-G6) decomposing gain into G_plan + G_scaffold + G_graph + G_patch + G_replan. This is explicitly a position paper: no prototype, no empirical data; the author openly acknowledges SGH degenerates to single-ready-unit when the planner produces a linear chain, and estimates that 60-70% of real tasks are essentially linear.
key_findings: Agent Loop's limitation is not weak prompting but a structural |U|<=1 scheduler property—adding planner or scaffold does not change scheduler class, only a real DAG does.
OpenAI parallel tool_calls and async operations produce |U|>1 but with non-deterministic P, so they are not structural parallelism.
Survey of 70 open-source agent projects: 60% use Agent Loop pattern; failure-loop behavior observed in 3/4 graph/flow projects vs 0/7 state-machine projects (small sample, qualitative).
Soundness is only conditional: Theorem 6.3 bounds correctness by prod_v p_v where p_v is per-node validation reliability; for LLM-based semantic validation p_v can be far below 1.
SGH explicitly excludes first_of competitive parallelism, recursive subgraph expansion, parent-chain rollback, and dynamic topology change—each violates a specific design principle.
Self-admission: when the planner generates a linear chain, SGH degenerates to a single-ready-unit scheduler; only bounded recovery, audit trail, and context isolation remain as benefits.
Author estimates 30-40% of agent tasks have natural parallelism; 60-70% are essentially linear.
limitations: Pure theoretical paper—no prototype, no benchmark, no empirical validation of any predicted gain.
Static DAG assumption fails for exploratory, creative, or investigation-then-fix tasks with emergent dependency structure.
Multi-ready-unit dispatch amplifies planner errors: a wrong DAG wastes tokens across parallel branches simultaneously.
Cold-start cost: new tasks without DAG templates require planner to build from scratch, overhead may dominate.
Implementation complexity estimated at 3,300-6,500 LOC—10x a naive Agent Loop.
Survey methodology is qualitative and subjective: 70 projects manually picked, inter-rater kappa=0.84, claims based on GitHub issue inspection.
Validation-gap independence assumption breaks when an LLM validates another LLM—correlated errors invalidate the product bound.

infra_impact.algorithm: Indirect: plan-version traces can serve as high-quality structured SFT/RL data for training agents; no direct training-recipe contribution.
infra_impact.kernel: N/A—no GPU kernel work. Indirect pressure: multi-ready-unit dispatch raises concurrent LLM request load, favoring serving stacks with strong prefix cache + continuous batching.
infra_impact.framework: Core impact: serving frameworks (vLLM/SGLang) need to treat each DAG node as an independent request while sharing plan-level prefix KV; agent frameworks need DAG validator, state-machine implementation, escalation FSM, append-only WAL, token-bucket rate limiter; competes directly with LangGraph's AgentExecutor layer.
infra_impact.llm: No model change required, but places strong demands on function-calling / JSON-mode / structured-output reliability since contract validation directly drives retry decisions; planner model quality is the critical bottleneck.
infra_impact.agent: Primary battleground. Provides a shared language (|U|, policy explicitness, plan versioning, escalation protocol) to quantitatively compare Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, LangGraph, CrewAI, TDP on the same axes, turning auditability/boundedness/parallelism into measurable design axes.
infra_impact.code: Offers a concrete 3,300-6,500 LOC reference architecture (DAG validator + concurrent scheduler + WAL + recovery engine + contract validator) that can be used as a blueprint for building structured agent runtimes.
infra_impact.cluster: N/A.

open_questions: What is the actual magnitude of G_graph on SWE-bench / WebArena / GAIA, and how does it interact with task parallelism density?
Can a parallelism-aware planner (SFT + RL on DAG-structured tasks) close the 15% gap WorfBench reports between sequence and graph planning?
Under correlated LLM-validator errors, what is the real distribution of Pr[all correct] vs the prod_v p_v lower bound?
Does the companion Evolutionary Graph Architecture paper (promised but unpublished) successfully relax the static-DAG constraint while preserving bounded termination?
Is there a dual-path routing sweet spot where simple tasks go to Agent Loop and complex tasks to SGH, and how do we cheaply classify task complexity upfront?
Can plan-level prefix cache in vLLM/SGLang give most of SGH's throughput benefit without the full static-DAG machinery?

papers.title: Scepsy: Serving Agentic Workflows Using Aggregate LLM Pipelines
papers.core_contribution: Scepsy introduces the Aggregate LLM Pipeline — an abstraction that exploits the empirical stability of each LLM's relative execution-time share across agentic workflow requests — to drive a joint (fractional-GPU, tensor-parallelism, replica-count) search with topology-aware placement, achieving up to 2.4× throughput and 27× lower latency than Kubernetes HPA, Aegaeon multiplexing, and Ayo on RAG+reranker and beam-search workloads.
papers.summary: Agentic workflows orchestrate multiple heterogeneous LLMs and tools with data-dependent control flow (branches, fan-out, loops), making their end-to-end latency highly unpredictable and causing severe GPU oversubscription on small-to-medium self-managed clusters. Existing approaches fall short: per-LLM autoscaling (K8s HPA, AIBrix) oscillates because it ignores inter-LLM coupling; multi-LLM multiplexing (AlpaServe, MuxServe, Aegaeon) uses fixed strategies ignoring workflow structure; workflow-aware systems (Parrot, Ayo, Autellix, JITServe) are restricted to single-LLM workflows and/or DSL-based programming. Scepsy's insight is that while absolute latencies vary wildly, the *fractional share* each LLM contributes to total execution time is ~4× more stable than absolute values. It compresses every LLM into an Aggregate LLM Pipeline stage parameterized by average invocation count n_m, average parallelism p_m, and per-LLM throughput/latency curves, yielding closed-form workflow-level predictors L_w = Σ L_m(λ·n_m)·n_m/p_m and T_w = min T_m/n_m. A joint scheduler searches over (fractional-GPU, tensor-parallelism, replica-count) pruned by latency-ratio heuristics and high-bandwidth-domain constraints, and a hierarchical placement algorithm maps allocations onto NVLink domains via Kubernetes + an extended NVIDIA device plugin + MPS. On a 16-GPU A6000 cluster, Scepsy delivers 1.2-2.4× higher throughput and 1.3-27× lower latency than K8s HPA, 2.5-7.3× throughput and 3.4-14.1× latency gains over Aegaeon, and 2.1-8.2× higher peak throughput than Ayo — with scheduler search time <35s for typical cluster sizes and <70s projected for 128 GPUs.
key_findings: Relative per-LLM execution-time share is ~4× more stable across workflow requests than absolute latencies — this empirical fact is the foundation of the entire Aggregate LLM Pipeline abstraction.

Simple analytical predictors (L_w, T_w) using aggregate statistics plus per-LLM T-L profiling give enough accuracy to drive GPU allocation decisions without re-simulating stochastic workflow execution.

Joint (fractional-GPU, tensor-parallelism, replica-count) search space is the minimal sufficient dimension set for co-optimizing throughput and latency; ablations show each dimension contributes differently per workload (fractional dominates RAG throughput gains; TP dominates beam-search latency gains).

Workflow-awareness at the allocation layer beats both workflow-agnostic multi-LLM multiplexing (Aegaeon) and single-LLM workflow-aware request scheduling (Ayo) — showing that these two optimization axes are complementary, not alternatives.
Topology-aware fractional placement is an engineering gap in existing GPU orchestrators; Scepsy fills it with an ahead-of-time hierarchical heuristic + extended NVIDIA device plugin + MPS isolation.

Large search spaces (~29M candidates for 5 LLMs × 16 GPUs × 10 fractions) become tractable via three pruning strategies — sort LLMs by latency ratio, contiguous allocation only, cap TP at high-bandwidth-domain size — reducing runtime to <35s.
limitations: Aggregate Pipeline mis-models workflows where fan-out spans different LLMs (treats it as serial), over-estimating latency; discussion proposes overlap-tracking fix but not implemented.

Single-LLM bimodal load distributions (same LLM playing multiple roles or shared across workflows) are fitted by a single aggregate and mis-allocated; proposed pattern-classification fix is unimplemented.
Relies on the assumption that non-LLM tool execution time is <few ms — invalid for agents with long-running tools (web search, code execution, visual processing, RL rollouts).
Requires a 'representative trace' for initial profiling; no online drift detection or incremental re-profiling mechanism.

Evaluation is small-scale (16× A6000 with only 2-lane NVLink pair + PCIe4 + 100G IB), not representative of H100/NVSwitch clusters where tensor-parallelism search space is much larger; 128-GPU numbers are micro-benchmark extrapolation only.
Tensor parallelism is hard-capped at the high-bandwidth domain size, sacrificing large cross-domain TP configurations.
Isolation relies on NVIDIA MPS static SM/memory limits, with no explicit QoS guarantees against noisy-neighbor effects.
Ayo baseline uses an older vLLM v0.2 for compatibility, which may under-represent Ayo's performance.

infra_impact.algorithm: N/A — no new training/inference algorithm; indirectly enables production deployment of inference-time-scaling methods like beam search and MCTS by making them cost-effective on modest clusters.
infra_impact.kernel: N/A — uses off-the-shelf vLLM/SGLang engines; no kernel modifications.
infra_impact.framework: Direct impact — introduces a workflow-aware orchestration layer between Kubernetes control plane and vLLM/SGLang engines, pushing K8s device plugins toward first-class fractional + topology-aware GPU resource models; complements per-LLM autoscaling stacks (AIBrix, llm-d, vLLM Production Stack) as a drop-in scheduler.
infra_impact.llm: N/A — model-architecture agnostic; supports any vLLM/SGLang-compatible model.
infra_impact.agent: Core impact — provides a zero-code-change GPU auto-allocation runtime for LangChain / LangGraph / AutoGen / Camel agents, filling a critical gap in self-managed agentic deployment stacks; complements request-level schedulers (Autellix, JITServe) which handle priority but not LLM-level allocation.
infra_impact.cluster: Introduces new scheduling requirements — fractional GPU × NVLink-domain placement — that Kubernetes schedulers and gang-schedulers (Kai-scheduler, Volcano) do not yet support natively.
infra_impact.code: Reference implementation (~25K LoC Python) planned to be open-sourced post-acceptance at github.com/anon/Scepsy; structurally reusable by AIBrix / llm-d / vLLM Production Stack.

open_questions: How to extend Aggregate LLM Pipeline to bimodal load distributions when a single LLM plays multiple roles across or within workflows?
How to incorporate long-running non-LLM tools (web search, code execution, RL rollouts) as first-class stages in the pipeline model?
How should scheduler behavior adapt when production traffic drifts away from the profiling trace distribution?
Does the 'relative share stability' assumption hold for highly adaptive ReAct-style agents where each turn may trigger a completely different tool/LLM chain?
How does the search space and pruning behavior scale on H100/NVSwitch clusters where TP-domain size is much larger than A6000's 2-lane NVLink pair?
Can a two-layer scheduler combining Scepsy's allocation-level decisions with Autellix-style request-level priority deliver additional gains?

papers.title: Seedance 2.0: Advancing Video Generation for World Complexity
papers.core_contribution: Seedance 2.0 is ByteDance's native multi-modal audio-video joint generation foundation model based on a dual-branch MMDiT architecture; supports 4 input modalities (text/image/video/audio) and 22 multi-modal tasks (20 supported, 7 exclusive), achieving #1 on Arena.AI T2V (Elo 1450) and I2V (Elo 1449) leaderboards while outperforming Sora 2 Pro / Veo 3.1 / Kling 3.0 / Wan 2.6 across every evaluated dimension.
papers.summary: Seedance 2.0 is a release/evaluation report (not a technical paper) for ByteDance's new native multi-modal AV generation model deployed via Doubao / Jimeng / Volcano Engine. The paper inherits its dual-branch MMDiT architecture from Seedance 1.5 pro: video and audio each have a DiT branch, joined by a cross-modal joint module for tight AV synchronization, with text injected via cross-attention/AdaLN. Outputs are 4-15s native 480p/720p video plus binaural multi-track audio (background + ambient + dialogue). Reference-to-Video supports up to 3 video clips, 9 images, and 3 audio clips. The Seedance 2.0 Fast variant uses multi-stage distillation for low-latency. Evaluation uses SeedVideoBench 2.0 (30 motion sub-categories, 17 audio sub-categories, 22 multi-modal tasks) plus Arena.AI third-party blind testing. Seedance 2.0 ranks first on every dimension across T2V/I2V/R2V, with the largest gaps on audio (Audio Quality 3.67 vs runner-up 3.36), motion stability (Motion Quality 3.75 vs 3.10), and multi-modal task coverage (20/22 vs next best 13/22). Seven tasks (VFX/Creative reference + Continuation/Extension) are exclusive. The paper does NOT disclose architecture / training / data / compute details — those are inherited from the predecessor Seedance 1.5 pro paper.
key_findings: Only model leading on every dimension in T2V (6 dims), I2V (6 dims), and R2V (5 dims) simultaneously
Audio is the largest moat: T2V audio satisfaction rate (≥4 score) is 51-68% vs all competitors below 35%
Multi-modal task coverage: 20/22 R2V tasks supported (next best Vidu Q2 Pro at 13/22); 7 exclusive tasks (VFX/Creative ref + Continuation/Extension)
Arena.AI Elo 1450 (T2V) / 1449 (I2V) at 720p output beats 1080p competitors — perceptual quality independent of resolution
Motion Quality fine-grained: 29 out of 30 subcategories first; Editing Rhythm 4.21, Multi-Entity Match 4.43
Seedance 2.0 Fast variant via multi-stage distillation inherits 1.5 pro's >=10x acceleration recipe
Honest about weaknesses: video extension (1.93 vs Veo 3.1's 2.78), multi-subject consistency, complex editing, Chinese opera audio prompt-following
limitations: Zero architecture / training / data / compute disclosure — paper is a release statement, not a tech report
Only compared against commercial closed-source models (Sora 2, Veo 3.1, Kling, Wan 2.6, Vidu); ignores open-source SOTA (HunyuanVideo 1.5, Step-Video, Open-Sora)
Video extension task still trails Veo 3.1 by 0.85 points
Seedance 2.0 Fast variant has no quality-vs-latency curve disclosed
Most evaluation scores from internal SeedVideoBench 2.0; only Arena.AI Elo is third-party verification
No information on inference cost (GPU-seconds per generation), making cost-quality comparison impossible

infra_impact.algorithm: Validates GRPO family (DanceGRPO, RewardDance) on visual diffusion at production scale; multi-stage distillation (Hyper-SD/RayFlow/MeanFlow) is essential toolkit for low-latency video gen.
infra_impact.kernel: Dual-branch MMDiT with cross-modal joint attention requires kernels handling asymmetric cross-modal sequence lengths; long video + binaural audio joint denoising stresses attention kernels (SLA-style sparse-linear attention becomes more valuable).
infra_impact.framework: AV joint inference at 4-15s x 720p x binaural is a new state-management challenge; multi-modal reference inputs (3 video + 9 image + 3 audio) require new conditioning scheduling primitives.
infra_impact.llm: Counter-evidence to 'LLM-as-foundation' narrative: best-path for video generation remains dedicated MMDiT, not extending an LLM context window to all modalities.
infra_impact.agent: Raises ceiling for video-generation agents (script -> storyboard -> video -> editing); 22 R2V tasks expand the tool catalog available to creative agents.
infra_impact.code: Closed-source — only API access via Doubao / Jimeng / Volcano Engine; no modeling code, ckpt, or training script available.

open_questions: Cross-modal joint module implementation: token-level vs block-level vs shared attention?
Seedance 2.0 Fast NFE count and quality degradation curve
Total parameter count, training tokens/clips, GPU-hours
Dense vs MoE backbone?
Video VAE spatial/temporal compression ratio
Will weights be open-sourced (historical answer for ByteDance: unlikely)?

papers.title: Prefill-as-a-Service: KVCache of Next-Generation Models Could Go Cross-Datacenter
papers.core_contribution: 提出 PrfaaS 架构, 利用 hybrid attention 把 KV 缩 13x 后, 通过 selective offloading + dual-timescale 调度实现跨数据中心的 PD 解耦, 在 1T 模型上比同构 PD 快 54% 同时只占 100Gbps 链路的 13%。
papers.summary: PD 解耦受限于 KVCache 传输带宽, 传统 dense 模型 32K 时单实例 60 Gbps 把部署锁在单 RDMA 集群内。Hybrid attention (Kimi Linear/KDA/SWA/Lightning) 把 KV throughput 缩到 3-8 Gbps, 让 commodity Ethernet 成为可行选项, 但 bursty 流量 + 长度偏态 + prefix 不均 + 链路抖动让 naive 全外部化失败。PrfaaS-PD 用三件事把『理论可行』变『生产可用』: (1) 长度阈值 t 的 selective routing — 仅 l>t 走 PrfaaS 集群, 短请求留在本地 PD; (2) Hybrid Prefix Cache Pool — 线性 attn 状态 (request-level) 与 full attn KV (block-level) 同物理池但区分 prefix-cache (复用) 与 transfer-cache (跨簇丢弃) 块; (3) 双时间尺度调度 — 短时按 BW 利用率与 prefix 命中决策路由, 长时迁移 PD 集群内 N_p/N_d 比例并重算 t。Throughput model (Eq.3-6) 把端到端写成 $\Lambda_{\max}=\min(\Theta_{prfaas}/p, \Theta_{pdp}/(1-p), \Theta_{pdd})$, 二维网格搜索找最优。1T 内部 hybrid 模型 + 32 H200 PrfaaS + 64 H20 PD + 100Gbps VPC 案例下: t=19.4K, N_p=3, N_d=5, ~50% 请求被卸载, Λ_max=3.24 req/s, 比同构 PD (96 H20) 快 54%, P90 TTFT 降 64%, 出口仅占 13 Gbps。
key_findings: Hybrid attention 在 32K 长度下把 dense 模型的 60 Gbps Φ_kv 缩到 3-8 Gbps (13x 缩减), 使跨 DC commodity Ethernet 首次成为 PD 解耦的可行通道
模型架构便宜是必要不充分: bursty + 长度偏态 + prefix 不均 + 链路抖动让 naive 全外部化失败, 必须配合 selective offloading + bandwidth-aware 调度
Throughput model Λ_max=min(Θ_prfaas/p, Θ_pdp/(1-p), Θ_pdd), 最优 t 由 Eq.7 (路径平衡) 给出, 最优 N_p/N_d 由 Eq.8 (产消平衡) 给出, 二维网格搜索可解
Hybrid Prefix Cache Pool 把 request-level 线性状态与 block-level 全 attn KV 共享物理池, 区分 prefix vs transfer 块语义, 是把 vLLM hybrid manager 扩展到跨簇的关键工程组件
Dual-timescale 调度: 短时按 BW utilization + prefix cache 联合决策, 长时按流量重新分配 N_p/N_d 并 re-solve t
1T 模型案例 (32 H200 + 64 H20 + 100Gbps VPC): t=19.4K → 49.6% 请求路由到 PrfaaS, 跨 DC 出口仅 13 Gbps, Λ_max=3.24 比同构 (2.11) 快 54%, P90 TTFT 从 9.73s → 3.51s (降 64%)
naive 异构 (无调度) 仅 1.16x over 同构, PrfaaS 多得的 32% 全部来自调度, 证明『调度即架构』
等成本 ($) 下 PrfaaS 优势缩减为 ~15%, 但 P90 TTFT 改善仍显著
limitations: 评估只用单一内部 1T 模型 + 单一 log-normal 分布 (mean ~27K, output 1024); 没有 ShareGPT/Loogle/agentic 等公开 benchmark 复现
Throughput model 用 L_avg 抽象掉了 prefix cache 命中与 routing 的耦合
13 Gbps 是平均值, 突发模式下打爆 100 Gbps 链路的概率与降级曲线未量化
跨 DC 链路抖动/中断时的 fallback 与 transfer-cache 阻塞 PD-D 的处理仅一句话带过

强绑定 hybrid stack 路线 (linear+full 交替); 注意不是 dense vs hybrid 的简单对立——dense MHA 早已被 GQA/MQA/MLA 替代, 论文里所谓 dense baseline 本身就是 GQA。真正风险是 hybrid stack 被更激进方案 (native sparse attention / 2-bit KV 量化 / Mamba 全替代) 取代
跨 DC OPEX 通常按 95th-percentile 计费, 论文用 mean 13 Gbps 报告是低估了真实成本
实现 in-house 未开源, 全局 KVCache manager 的一致性 / 高可用方案细节缺失

infra_impact.algorithm: N/A — 不是算法/训练改动, 但间接受益于 hybrid attention 训练侧 (KDA/Lightning/GDN 等) 持续发展, 反过来给模型设计提供『优化 KV transfer 体积』的部署侧目标
infra_impact.kernel: 需要 paged-KV 内核支持 hybrid block layout (linear-state + full-attn KV 同物理池) 与 transfer-cache 块语义; vLLM hybrid KVCache manager 已部分覆盖, 其他引擎需要适配
infra_impact.framework: 直接重塑 PD 解耦的部署边界——把 Mooncake『KV 一等公民』扩到跨 DC; 给 vLLM/SGLang/Dynamo 提了多 cluster 多 DC 的新部署 footprint, dual-timescale scheduler 可能成为多 DC 推理标配
infra_impact.llm: 强化『hybrid attention 是大模型必由之路』, 推动 model designer 把 inter-cluster KV 体积纳入设计目标; Kimi Linear / MiMo / Qwen3.5 / Ring-2.5-1T / Nemotron-3 Nano 等都受益
infra_impact.agent: agentic workload 是多轮短增量 prefill + 高 prefix 命中, 正是 PrfaaS 目标场景之一; 跨 DC 部署使 agent 平台可在低成本 region 弹性扩容长上下文 prefill
infra_impact.code: N/A — 实现未开源; Mooncake repo (Moonshot AI 已开源 KVCache transfer engine) 是下游工程的最近参照, 跨 DC 版本待发布

open_questions: 若 hybrid stack 路线被替代 (native sparse attention / 模型级 2-bit KV 量化 / Mamba/RWKV 全替代), PrfaaS 的'刚好够用'窗口是否再次错位?
跨 DC 链路抖动/中断时 transfer-cache 卡在中间是否触发 prefill 重做? PD-D 等待超时如何处理?
t 与 N_p/N_d 的实时调整算法收敛性: 是否存在'队列爆→提 t→短请求踢回→PD-P 撞墙→降 t'的振荡?
PrfaaS 多租户隔离: 跨租户 prefix cache 命中是隐私风险吗?
全局 KVCache manager 的一致性 / 容灾方案 (Raft? gossip? 单点)?
等成本下 ~15% ROI 是否足以推动企业改造现有 PD 部署?

papers.title: Speculative Speculative Decoding
papers.core_contribution: 提出 Speculative Speculative Decoding (SSD) 框架，通过在 verification 进行时预测 outcome 并预计算 speculation (speculation cache)，将 drafting 与 verification 完全并行化。优化算法 Saguaro 平均比最优 SD baseline 快 30%。
papers.summary: Speculative decoding (SD) 虽然加速了 LLM 推理，但本身仍受限于 speculation → verification 的顺序依赖。SSD 将 draft model 部署在独立硬件上，在 verification 并行期间为最可能的 verification outcomes 预计算 speculation 并存入 cache。Saguaro 算法通过三个关键优化解决 SSD 挑战：(1) geometric fan-out cache topology 最优分配 cache budget；(2) Saguaro sampling 平衡 acceptance rate 与 cache hit rate；(3) batch-size-adaptive fallback 策略。实验在 Llama-3.1-70B (4×H100) + Llama-3.2-1B draft (1×H100) 上，平均比 SD 快 30%，最高达 AR 的 5× 加速。
key_findings: SSD 将 SD 的顺序 speculate→verify 流程并行化，draft model 在独立硬件上与 verification 并发运行
Geometric fan-out cache topology (Theorem 12) 最优分配 cache budget，前端位置分配更多 fan-out
通过 draft logits top-k 预测 bonus token，greedy decoding 下命中率约 90%
Cache miss rate 实证遵循 power law，使优化问题有 closed-form 解
Saguaro sampling 通过 logit bonus C 单调提升 cache hit rate
Fallback 策略应随 batch size 自适应切换：临界 b* 以下用 just-in-time，以上用 random token
limitations: 需要额外 1 GPU 用于 draft model，增加硬件成本
Cache hit rate 在高温度采样和大 batch size 下显著下降
最佳效果在 batch=1 greedy decoding，大 batch 优势衰减
仅验证 Llama-3 和 Qwen-3 两个模型家族
未与 EAGLE-3 等最新 draft architecture 实验验证
大 batch 下 fallback 到 random token 的策略质量有待提升

infra_impact.algorithm: Saguaro sampling scheme 为 SD 提供新 sampling 维度——acceptance rate vs 预测准确率的 trade-off
infra_impact.kernel: 不需要自定义 kernel，但多 outcome 并行 KV cache 管理可受益于 paged attention 优化
infra_impact.framework: 直接影响 serving 框架的 SD 实现——disaggregated draft model 部署架构
infra_impact.llm: 与模型架构无关，但 acceptance rate 受 draft-target 分布差异影响
infra_impact.agent: 对 latency-sensitive agent 场景直接受益，单 token 延迟降低 30%

open_questions: SSD 与 EAGLE-3 等 draft architecture 组合后能否进一步提升？
如何将 SSD 集成到 vLLM/SGLang 的 continuous batching 框架中？
大 batch size 下有没有比 random token 更好的 fallback 策略？
多节点 TP 场景下 draft-target 间通信延迟对 SSD 的影响？
Power-law cache hit rate 假设在哪些分布下会失效？

papers.title: Attention Residuals
papers.core_contribution: 把 PreNorm 的恒定权重残差累加替换为'softmax-attention 沿深度加权求和'：每层学一个伪 query w_ℓ 对所有先前层输出 v_i 做 softmax 选择性聚合 h_ℓ = Σ α_{i→ℓ} v_i。Block-AttnRes 训练 overhead < 4%, 已用于 Kimi Linear 48B-A3B (GPQA +7.5)。
papers.summary: AttnRes 解决 PreNorm 的根本问题: 标准残差 h_ℓ = h_{ℓ-1} + f(h_{ℓ-1}) 用固定权重 1 累加, 导致 hidden 范数 O(L) 增长且早期层贡献被稀释。AttnRes 用 softmax 替代固定权重: α_{i→ℓ} = exp(w_ℓᵀ RMSNorm(k_i)) / Σ exp(...), 每层都能 input-dependent 选择从哪些早期层取信息 (时间-深度对偶)。Block-AttnRes 是工程化版本: 只在 block 边界做 (每 block_size//2 层一次), block 内仍标准 +; 实际放在 attn 子层之前和 MLP 子层之前各一次. 新参数仅 2L·d (相对 FFN 可忽略), 必须 zero-init 让起始退化为均匀平均. 两阶段在线 softmax 合并支持 cache + partial block. 关键消融: softmax > sigmoid, single-head depth-attn > multi-head, RMSNorm on V 必需, DenseFormer 固定标量无效. 训练动态: hidden norm bounded periodic (vs baseline 单调增长), 梯度 norm 更均匀. Scaling law: Block-AttnRes 节省 ~1.25x compute. 已被 Kimi Linear 48B-A3B 使用 (1.4T, Muon + WSD), GPQA +7.5, < 4% overhead.
key_findings: 核心方程 h_ℓ = Σ_i α_{i→ℓ} v_i, softmax 取代固定权重
Block-AttnRes 注入点: 每 transformer 层 2 次 (attn-pre + MLP-pre), block 内仍 +
新参数仅 2L·d (Linear(d,1) 伪 query + RMSNorm 每注入点)
Pseudo-query MUST zero-init (否则训练不稳)
Softmax 必胜: sigmoid 或 fixed-scalar (DenseFormer) 都无效
Single-head depth-attention > multi-head (无收益增 params)
Hidden norm: baseline 单调增长 vs AttnRes bounded periodic (block 边界 reset)
Gradient norm 沿深度更均匀 → 早期层得到更强信号
Scaling law: Block-AttnRes 节省 ~1.25x compute (matches baseline 在更少 compute)
Kimi Linear 48B-A3B 实战: GPQA +7.5, training overhead < 4%, I/O ~5.5d/token/layer
Block-size=6 sublayers 是 Kimi Linear 配置 (= 3 transformer 层一个 block)
limitations: 新参数虽少(每层仅一个d维向量)但增加了L^2级别的注意力计算
Block AttnRes的block大小是超参数，需要调优
Pipeline parallelism下仍有<4% overhead
仅在MoE架构上验证，dense模型效果未知

infra_impact.algorithm: 将time-depth duality形式化，用attention替代residual连接是新的研究方向
infra_impact.kernel: N/A — 计算为标准矩阵乘法
infra_impact.framework: 需要framework支持cross-layer communication，pipeline parallelism需cache-based策略
infra_impact.llm: 证明标准residual connection是LLM性能瓶颈之一，Block AttnRes是drop-in替换
infra_impact.agent: 间接受益：模型质量提升

open_questions: AttnRes 在 100B+ dense 模型上的扩展性？目前仅 MoE Kimi Linear 验证
Pseudo-query 维度 = d 是必需还是可压缩 (low-rank)？
Block size 6 是否对其他 scale 也最优？
Multi-head depth attention 真的没用还是消融 setup 不充分？
AttnRes 与 dynamic depth (early exit) 的结合潜力？

papers.title: Efficient LLM Serving for Agentic Workflows: A Data Systems Perspective
papers.core_contribution: Helium reframes batch agentic LLM workflows as database query plans with LLMs as first-class operators, combining proactive KV/prompt caching, a Templated Radix Tree encoding both static prefix structure and operator dependencies, and a cost-based cache-aware scheduler that produces nested operator sequences exploiting two levels of prefix sharing, achieving up to 1.56x speedup over KVFlow.
papers.summary: Existing LLM serving engines (vLLM, SGLang) optimize single calls and treat workflow structure as invisible; orchestration frameworks (LangGraph, AgentScope) wrap LLMs as black-box UDFs and are blind to KV cache and prefill/decode asymmetry. Helium closes this gap by borrowing classical data-systems machinery: DSL -> DAG -> query optimizer (plan pruning + CSE + CacheFetch substitution) -> Templated Radix Tree -> cost-based cache-aware scheduler -> vLLM workers via IPC. The TRT encodes static prompt prefixes AND operator dependencies in one structure, enabling a nested-sequence schedule where inner sequences share per-query context and outer sequences share static system prompts, a dual-prefix reuse impossible for single-prefix schedulers. Proactive caching pre-fills static prefixes once and pins them across batches; a global prompt cache replaces repeat deterministic operators with CacheFetch retrievals. Evaluated on five primitive workflows (MapRed, Debate, Reflect, Iterative, Parallel) on MMLU/TAT-QA/Amazon with Qwen3-8B on 2xH100, Helium beats vLLM by 38-101x, LangGraph by up to 1.83x, Parrot by 2.21x, and KVFlow by 1.56x. On a realistic 88-operator Trading workflow it is 1.34x over KVFlow and 39.5x over vLLM. Scheduling quality: 0.9% average optimality gap vs MILP, 16x tighter than SGLang-style LSPF. Ablations surprisingly show plan pruning (-23.35%) contributes more than any cache mechanism.
key_findings: Plan pruning via CSE is the single largest contributor (-23.35% if removed), larger than all cache mechanisms combined; unpruned redundant operators pollute the TRT and cascade into worse schedules.

Templated Radix Tree + nested-sequence schedule is the core barrier: dual-layer prefix reuse (static system prompt + per-query context) cannot be achieved by any single-prefix scheduler; LSPF's hit rate is only 1.5% above Random while Helium's is 32.9% above LSPF.
Scheduling quality is within 0.9% of MILP optimum on average vs 14.5% for LSPF and 16.3% for Random, proving global workflow-level visibility beats local online prefix heuristics.

High cache hit rate does not imply fast execution: vLLM on Qwen3-14B matches Helium's 42.9% hit rate but is 38x slower because it executes sequentially; scheduling must translate hit rate into effective batch expansion.
TRT metadata footprint scales with workflow structure not batch size: 552 KiB vs SGLang RadixCache's 14.8 MiB at 16 branches (27x advantage).
Helium's advantage widens with batch size (8 to 80) and with model size (8B to 32B), confirming the thesis that workflow-level redundancy grows with workload scale.
limitations: DAG abstraction cannot natively express dynamic control flow (conditional loops, runtime-dependent fan-out, MCTS rollouts); paper explicitly flags this for future work.
Closed world: no remote API/tool calls, no model heterogeneity (all agents share one base LLM), no quality-aware approximation.
Prompt cache and CacheFetch require deterministic operators (greedy sampling), making the 13.56% prompt-cache contribution vanish for temperature>0 exploration workloads like ToT/GoT.
Requires offline profiling of output-length statistics per operator; brittle for reasoning LLMs with high CoT length variance.
Evaluated only on single-node 2xH100; no multi-node scheduling, no cross-host KV migration, no integration with disaggregated prefill/decode.
On Qwen3-14B the proactive KV advantage shrinks because pinned prefixes compete with active-request KV blocks under HBM pressure.
Users must rewrite workflows in Helium's custom DSL; no drop-in LangGraph/AgentScope compatibility.

infra_impact.algorithm: Repositions agent workflows as algebraic query plans amenable to CSE, plan rewriting, and cost-based ordering; opens door to treating prompt selection and sampling as algebraic rewrites.
infra_impact.kernel: No new kernels; relies on vLLM PagedAttention. Augments vLLM with a pin mechanism for KV blocks tied to static prefixes, which future kernel work could specialize.
infra_impact.framework: Biggest impact layer. Introduces TRT data structure, proactive vs passive cache taxonomy, and operator-level scheduling granularity; directly contests Parrot, SGLang, KVFlow, and LangGraph design choices.
infra_impact.llm: Orthogonal to model architecture; any decoder-only LLM with standard KV cache works. Does require deterministic sampling for prompt-cache hits.
infra_impact.agent: Provides a Python DSL that records workflow as symbolic DAG, enabling global optimization; could become compilation target for LangGraph/AgentScope if they expose structure rather than UDF black boxes.
infra_impact.cluster: N/A at single-node scope; cross-node KV migration is out of scope.
infra_impact.hardware: N/A; no hardware-specific optimizations, though HBM capacity directly determines how much pinned prefix fits.
infra_impact.code: Open-source prototype at github.com/mlsys-io/helium_demo, built on vLLM v0.16.0 with IPC-based worker engines.

open_questions: How to extend TRT to dynamic control flow (conditional loops, runtime fan-out) without losing the compile-time optimization benefits?
Can prompt caching be made sampling-aware via approximate semantic equivalence (embedding similarity) or confidence-weighted caching?
How does the cost model and scheduler generalize to multi-node deployments where pinned KV cannot be shared across hosts, and to disaggregated prefill/decode settings?
Can Helium's batch-centric approach be unified with online serving (mixed workloads of periodic batches + interactive requests)?
In heterogeneous-model pipelines (router sending easy queries to small models, hard ones to big), how to reason about prefix sharing when KV is not transferable across model architectures?
Under high HBM pressure (14B+ models, long pinned prefixes), how should hierarchical KV offload (HBM/DRAM/NVMe) be orchestrated with workflow-aware scheduling?

papers.title: DualPath: Breaking the Storage Bandwidth Bottleneck in Agentic LLM Inference
papers.core_contribution: DualPath introduces dual-path KV-Cache loading in PD-disaggregated inference, leveraging idle decode-engine storage NICs to break prefill-side I/O bottleneck, achieving up to 1.87x offline and 1.96x online throughput improvement.

papers.title: Kimi K2.5: Visual Agentic Intelligence
papers.core_contribution: 把 K2 的 1.04T MoE LLM (61L · d=7168 · MLA · 384E top-8 + 1 shared) 复用为多模态主干，前接 27L MoonViT-3D 视觉塔；通过早期融合 10:90 vision:text + zero-vision SFT + PARL Agent Swarm + DEP infra (90% text-only throughput) 实现高效多模态训练。
papers.summary: Kimi K2.5 的设计哲学是 LLM 主干别动 (完全继承 Kimi K2 的 MLA + MoE)，把所有创新放到对接层 (MoonViT-3D + patchmerger MLP) 和训练管线上。LLM: 61L × d=7168, MLA (q_lora=1536, kv_lora=512, qk_nope/rope/v=128/64/128), 384 routed top-8 + 1 shared expert (hidden=2048 SwiGLU), vocab=163840, YaRN factor=64 → 262K context. Vision: MoonViT-3D 27L × d=1152, 16 heads × head_dim=72 (SigLIP-SO-400M init), NaViT packing, 2×2 spatial merge + 4× temporal pool, patchmerger MLP → 7168. 训练 4 阶段: K2 15T text → ViT 1T → Joint 15T (vision:text=10:90) → Mid-train 32K→262K. 后训练: zero-vision SFT → joint multimodal SFT → joint multimodal RL (按 ability 组织, token-level log-ratio clip + KL penalty, MuonClip optimizer, rule + vision-specific + GRM rewards, Toggle for budget control). Agent 变体走 PARL: 编排器 RL 训练，子 agent 冻结，3-4.5x 延迟降低。
key_findings: LLM 主干 = K2 verbatim: 61L · d=7168 · MLA · 384E top-8 + 1 shared, expert hidden=2048
MLA latent rank: q_lora=1536, kv_lora=512 → KV cache 仅 576 floats/token (vs MHA 数千)
MoE: sigmoid scoring + noaux_tc top-8 + routed_scaling_factor=2.827
YaRN factor=64: 4K → 262K context (beta_fast=32, beta_slow=1)
Vision tower 相对小 (27×1152 vs 主干 7168), 靠 NaViT + 2×2 merge + 4× tpool 控制 token 数
Early fusion 10:90 vision:text 比常见 50:50 更好
Zero-vision SFT 比 text+vision SFT 更好: 文本 SFT + IPython 图像操作代理足够激活视觉能力
Vision RL 反向提升 text 能力 (GPQA +2.1, MMLU-Pro +1.7)
DEP (Decoupled Encoder Process) 让多模态训练 ~90% throughput of text-only
PARL Agent Swarm: 3-4.5x 延迟下降, 子 agent 冻结 + 编排器 RL
Toggle: budget vs full-length 交替, ~25-30% token 节省
limitations: Vision encoder仍基于SigLIP初始化，可能限制视觉理解上限
Agent Swarm需要额外subagent inference开销
OSWorld/WebArena仍落后Claude Opus 4.5
SimpleQA(36.9)相比Gemini 3 Pro(72.1)差距显著——事实性知识仍是短板

infra_impact.algorithm: Zero-vision SFT是重要方法论贡献——减少对标注视觉数据的依赖；PARL为多agent训练提供新范式
infra_impact.kernel: N/A — 使用标准vision-language kernel
infra_impact.framework: DEP(Decoupled Encoder Process)达90%多模态训练效率；Agent Swarm的context sharding理念可启发推理框架设计
infra_impact.llm: 证明early fusion + low vision ratio是多模态预训练最优策略
infra_impact.agent: Agent Swarm开创性地将multi-agent orchestration作为RL目标训练，PARL是可复制的方法论

open_questions: mid-train 'Table 3 500B→200B' 究竟是什么含义？两个子阶段还是课程？
zero-vision SFT 能否泛化到没有强联合预训的小模型？
PARL 子 agent 冻结策略，能否在更复杂多步任务上保持？
Toggle 的 ρ-percentile 选择对结果敏感度如何？
MLA 在 K2 vs K2.5 是否完全一致？

papers.title: ThunderAgent: A Simple, Fast and Program-Aware Agentic Inference System
papers.core_contribution: Introduces the Agentic Program as a first-class scheduling unit persisting across LLM calls and tool executions, plus a program-aware scheduler (global waiting queue, state-aware pause/restore, provably optimal exponential time-decay caching function) and lifecycle-aware tool resource manager, delivering 1.5-3.6x serving and 1.8-3.9x RL rollout throughput gains for multi-turn agent workloads.
papers.summary: Current agent serving systems loosely pair an LLM engine (vLLM/SGLang) with a tool orchestrator (Kubernetes), each making decisions at per-request granularity and hence oblivious to workflow-level state. On 8xH100 with GLM-4.6 running SWE-Agent / OpenHands / ToolOrchestra, this causes three systemic failures: (1) KV-cache thrashing amplifies end-to-end latency by up to 7.14x as the tool-wait KV is evicted and must be reprefilled; (2) KV-aware routing pins workflows to fixed nodes, causing up to 51% cross-node memory imbalance over 90-minute rollouts; (3) completed workflows leak Docker sandboxes and ports, driving disk usage to system capacity. ThunderAgent solves all three by lifting each workflow into an Agentic Program tuple <ID, c, T, L, tau, s> (identifier, context tokens, tool environment set, node placement, execution phase R/A, status Active/Paused/Terminated) that the scheduler treats as a first-class object. On top, a cost model decomposes STP into decode+prefill (productive) vs recompute+unused+caching (wasted); the scheduler periodically (every delta-t) checks per-backend thrashing condition C_total < sum(c_p) and pauses/restores programs using scores that strictly prioritize Acting over Reasoning states and shorter contexts (shortest-first provably minimizes the quadratic recompute cost). A time-decay function f(t) discounts Acting programs' effective occupation; the authors prove via a Cauchy functional equation that exponential decay exp(-lambda*t) is the unique optimal form under the memoryless assumption. A global program-aware waiting queue replaces pin-to-node routing (lossless, since paused KV is already evicted), and a release-signal-driven GC with async environment preparation manages tool resources. Evaluations across coding (SWE-Bench), computer-use (OpenHands), routing (ToolOrchestra) and scientific discovery (ScienceAgentBench) agents show 1.48-3.58x serving and 1.79-3.92x RL-rollout throughput improvements, plus 4.2x disk savings. Importantly, appendix experiments show KV offloading (LMCache) and PD disaggregation - both winners for chatbots - actually hurt throughput in agent workloads, narrowing the legitimate design space.
key_findings: KV-cache thrashing is the #1 bottleneck for agent serving: per-request scheduling ignores cross-turn prefix reuse, amplifying end-to-end latency by up to 7.14x.
Session-pinned KV-aware routing (vLLM) is harmful for agents: two-node DP rollout sees 51% peak memory imbalance and >20% divergence for 37 of 90 minutes.

Shortest-first eviction is provably optimal: Cost_recompute is proportional to c_i^2 (quadratic in context length), and the convex super-additive objective yields strict optimality for evicting smallest-c programs.

Exponential decay f(t)=e^{-lambda*t} is the only admissible caching discount function under memoryless tool latencies; the proof uses the Cauchy functional equation f(t+delta)=f(t)f(delta) with boundary conditions.
High KV cache hit rate does not imply high throughput: Continuum's TTL-pin achieves higher hit rates but lower throughput than ThunderAgent due to inflated caching cost.
PD disaggregation degrades agent workloads: partitioning HBM into prefill-only / decode-only pools triggers thrashing at much lower concurrency because agent contexts grow monotonically.
KV offloading to CPU/SSD via LMCache cannot rescue agent serving: PCIe bandwidth is insufficient for high-frequency context switches.

Low-touch adoption: only three application-layer changes are required (tag requests with program_id, pass program_id to tool calls, POST /programs/release at termination) while keeping the OpenAI API surface unchanged.
limitations: The memoryless assumption underlying optimal exponential decay does not hold for heavy-tailed remote APIs (Appendix C shows p99 in the hundreds of seconds); optimal decay form for Pareto-tailed tools is an open question.
Shortest-first eviction offers no fairness guarantee - short programs can starve while long ones are never paused; production deployment requires a priority or aging mechanism.
The tool resource GC depends on applications correctly sending an explicit release signal; forgetting it degrades back to Kubernetes-style timeout GC.
Only single-model workloads evaluated; multi-tenant multi-model routing is not addressed.
Not compared end-to-end against concurrent 2026 agent-serving systems (MegaFlow, RollArt, AgentRL, VerlTool) - only related-work discussion.
Scheduling is centralized with a global queue; scalability beyond tens of nodes is unverified (largest experiment is 2 nodes / 16 GPUs).
Requires backends to support pause/restore (abort_request) semantics and the DP (not PD) architecture - binding it away from some existing deployments.
Hysteresis window is collapsed to lambda_min=lambda_max=1 based on shared-prompt buffering assumption; may oscillate when system prompts are short or workflows heterogeneous.

infra_impact.algorithm: Directly benefits asynchronous RL training by raising rollout throughput 1.8-3.9x, reducing policy lag and improving convergence speed and final policy quality for agent RL (PPO/GRPO-style).
infra_impact.kernel: N/A - operates entirely at the scheduling layer and is orthogonal to FlashAttention, FP8 GEMM, or other kernel-level optimizations.
infra_impact.framework: Positions as the next generation of agent-aware middleware on top of vLLM/SGLang; directly competes with or extends Autellix, Continuum, Sutradhara, and MegaFlow. Highly likely to be absorbed into vLLM router and SGLang Gateway in 2026.
infra_impact.llm: Model-agnostic; any multi-turn + tool-using model benefits. Demonstrated on GLM-4.6 MoE.
infra_impact.agent: Major impact: agent frameworks (SWE-Agent, OpenHands, ToolOrchestra) gain a throughput-optimal runtime and will increasingly expose Reasoning vs Acting state explicitly as a de facto standard.
infra_impact.cluster: N/A - does not address networking or interconnect topology; lives above the node-level routing layer.
infra_impact.hardware: N/A - pure software; HBM-constrained accelerators (H20, A30) stand to gain more because thrashing triggers earlier.
infra_impact.code: Open-sourced at https://github.com/HaoKang-Timmy/ThunderAgent (arXiv HTML) / https://github.com/Agentic-Kinetics/ThunderAgent (arXiv abstract); program abstraction, global waiting queue, and time-decay modules are the key paths to study.

open_questions: Under heavy-tailed (Pareto / log-normal) tool latency distributions that violate memorylessness, what is the optimal decay function f(t)? Is it a power-law instead of exponential?
How to add fairness / aging to shortest-first eviction without sacrificing its optimality on Cost_recompute?
Can program-level scheduling be composed with sub-program sparse KV methods (ShadowKV, Pensieve, Quest, H2O) for compound gains?
Does the centralized global waiting queue scale to hundreds or thousands of nodes, or is hierarchical / decentralized scheduling required?
How should program routing and the six-tuple extend when multiple models share a cluster (multi-tenant multi-model serving)?
For sub-second local tools, does the periodic delta-t check introduce meaningful latency vs event-driven scheduling?

papers.title: Pancake: Hierarchical Memory System for Multi-Agent LLM Serving
papers.core_contribution: First multi-tier ANN memory system for multi-agent LLM serving, combining FSM-driven three-level index cache, cross-agent hybrid graph, and CPU-staged GPU-accelerated dynamic clustering to cut memory-op time from >82% to 3.2% with 4.29× average end-to-end throughput.
papers.summary: Pancake identifies agent memory operations as a new primary bottleneck in LLM serving (>82% of E2E time at scale) caused by three compounding issues: high-dimensional shell effects scattering in-place inserts across up to 175 clusters, linear coarse-search cost across N independent per-agent indexes, and CUDA's inability to support dynamic list expansion on GPU-resident hot clusters. The system introduces (1) Pattern-Driven Multi-Level Index Cache with L0/L1/L2 tiers and online FSM modeling of per-agent access sequences to enable workload-aware reordering, prefetching, and early termination (threshold alpha_et*d_agent); (2) Multi-Agent Hybrid Graph that stitches per-agent HNSW coarse indexes via portal edges with probability 1/ef_connect plus per-static-cluster agent profiles that reorder fine-search without duplicating the index; (3) GPU-CPU Coordinated Dynamic Index with a per-cluster CPU insertion buffer of size B_insert=128 (derived from Fig 8a hardware cross-over), fully asynchronous cluster migration (pre-alloc new GPU region, GPU-GPU + CPU-GPU copy overlap, release old), and on-GPU K-means splitting. Evaluated on Llama3.1-8B/70B + GPT-5 across UltraChat, UltraFeedback, Prm800k, Gsm8k, APIGen, AgentGym with four access patterns (One-Search-One-Insert, Step-Search-Then-Insert, Search-Then-Step-Insert, Search-Only). Pancake achieves 1.12x-26.18x (avg 4.29x) end-to-end speedup vs Mem-GPT/A-Mem/LangChain/LlamaIndex, 1.9x-4.2x vs standalone vector DBs (Quake/SPFresh/DiskANN) with an additional 2.2x from GPU coordination, >20x reduction in coarse-search cost, and <10.2% degradation at 20 concurrent agents.
key_findings: High-dimensional shell effect causes in-place insert to scatter semantically-related memory items across up to 175 clusters; 38-100% of those clusters are accessed <5% of the time.

Agent memory exhibits two compounding localities: intra-agent (same agent's items cluster tightly) and inter-request step-wise (same reasoning step across different requests clusters); per-agent dedicated cluster alone handles only the first.
Independent per-agent HNSW indexes incur linear coarse-search cost in agent count, exceeding 80% of E2E latency at 20 agents; hybrid graph with portal edges reduces this >20x.

CPU-GPU hardware cross-over for ANN search is around 256-512 vectors per cluster; Pancake uses this to set B_insert=128, matching CPU-buffer-search time to GPU cluster processing latency so CPU-side work is fully hidden behind GPU computation.
On-GPU cluster splitting via lightweight K-means eliminates a major CPU bottleneck; works because hot clusters (which need split) are already GPU-cached.

Ablation: multi-level cache + FSM alone gives 2.23x latency reduction, hybrid graph removes 11.6% similarity ops, agent profile removes additional 21.8%, GPU cache yields up to 1.92x (plateau at 5-15 GB).
limitations: Embedding model fixed to E5-1024; magic constants (alpha_et, alpha_ic, B_insert, N_p) may need re-tuning for different embedding dimensions or models.
Experiments limited to MS MARCO 8M-passage base (~8 GB scale); the paper motivates with >100 GB scale but does not validate at that size.
FSM construction uses online heuristic state-merging (d_merge threshold, max N_S states); no stress test on rapidly-drifting access patterns (e.g. agents switching tasks, exploratory workloads).
Async GPU-CPU migration window consistency (recall drift during in-flight search) is not quantified; multi-agent concurrent migrations and GPU memory fragmentation unanalyzed.
No SLO-aware or priority scheduling; alpha_et is a single global value rather than per-request.
Workloads are synthetic compositions of existing agent datasets; no real production traces from ChatGPT Memory / Claude Projects / Copilot.
FSM component lacks its own ablation; bundled with multi-level cache reporting, making individual contribution of access-pattern modeling unclear.
Single-node only; no multi-node / disaggregated (P-D split) serving story.
Code not yet released as of April 2026.

infra_impact.algorithm: Introduces FSM-based online modeling of agent access patterns as a lightweight O(d) per-access alternative to PCA/HMM; opens research path for learned memory access predictors in online RL and continual-learning agents.
infra_impact.kernel: Requires GPU K-means splitting kernel, multi-stream async GPU-CPU copy path, and a CPU-GPU top-k merge kernel; engineering load moderate but async migration stream scheduling is the tricky part.
infra_impact.framework: Defines a new 'agent memory manager' plane alongside KV cache management in LLM serving frameworks; provides drop-in adapters for LangChain, LlamaIndex, Mem-GPT; likely to be absorbed into vLLM/SGLang agent-mode extensions.
infra_impact.llm: No changes to LLM models themselves; directly benefits memory-augmented generation architectures (Mem-GPT, A-Mem, Mem0, G-Memory).
infra_impact.agent: Provides the first production-quality shared-memory backend for multi-agent frameworks (AutoGen, CAMEL, AI Town, LangGraph), replacing toy-grade memory stores with one that scales to 20 concurrent agents with <10% degradation.
infra_impact.cluster: N/A — single-node design; cross-node hybrid graph is identified as future work.
infra_impact.hardware: Exposes the CPU-GPU cross-over point (~256-512 vectors) as a first-class design parameter; Grace-Hopper / NVLink-C2C platforms would substantially boost async migration bandwidth.
infra_impact.code: No open-source release yet; implementation needs lock-free FSM table, HNSW with cross-graph portal edges, lock-free CPU insertion buffer, and epoch-based GPU memory reclamation.

open_questions: Does the FSM heuristic degrade under long chain-of-thought or nested sub-task agents with rapid pattern drift?
How does hybrid graph portal density scale at >100 GB vector DB with 50+ agents?
Where should Pancake's GPU cache live in P-D disaggregated LLM serving (prefill node, decode node, or dedicated memory GPU)?
Can the alpha_et verification mode actually tune itself online without additional training signals?
When will the code be open-sourced?

papers.title: AIConfigurator: Lightning-Fast Configuration Optimization for Multi-Framework LLM Serving
papers.core_contribution: A fast configuration optimizer for LLM serving that uses analytical modeling + Bayesian optimization to find near-optimal parallelism, batching, and framework configs in minutes instead of hours.

papers.title: Sutradhara: An Intelligent Orchestrator-Engine Co-design for Tool-based Agentic Inference
papers.core_contribution: 提出 orchestrator-engine co-design 架构 Sutradhara，通过 5 个 thin API 实现 prompt splitting（prefill-tool overlap）、streaming tool dispatch（decode-tool overlap）和 workload-aware KV cache management，在不修改模型架构的前提下将 agentic 推理的 FTR 延迟降低 15%。
papers.summary: Agentic LLM 应用通过迭代式 tool 调用完成复杂任务，但现有推理系统将 orchestrator 和 LLM engine 视为解耦的黑箱，导致三大性能瓶颈：tool 执行占据 30-80% FTR 延迟、串行编排浪费可利用的并行性、以及 LRU KV cache 驱逐在多请求并发时引发级联 thrashing。Sutradhara 提出协同设计方案，通过 submit_partial_prefill/extend_prefill/register_streaming_callback/tag_kv_blocks/set_reuse_priority 五个 API 使 orchestrator 向 engine 传递语义信息。三大优化包括：(1) prompt splitting 实现 prefill-tool 并行；(2) streaming JSON parser 在 decode 过程中增量分发 tool；(3) 语义标签优先级驱逐 + 请求到达时间优先调度。基于 vLLM v0.11.0 实现（3500 行），在 A100-80GB 上 Qwen3-14B/Gemma-12B 评估，tool-heavy 和 iteration-heavy 两种 trace 下一致性降低 FTR 10-15%、e2e 延迟 6-10%，不损失吞吐量。
key_findings: Tool 执行占 FTR 延迟的 30-80%（尾部），变异系数超过 100%，使基于预测的优化方案不可行
50-80% 的下一轮 prompt 不依赖 tool 输出，为 prefill-tool overlap 提供天然并行化机会
LRU KV cache 驱逐在 agentic 并发场景下导致级联 thrashing，语义优先级驱逐可有效缓解
Prompt Splitting 贡献 7.57% FTR 改进，Streaming Dispatch 额外贡献 4.2%
Co-design 方案仅需 3500 行代码改动，不修改模型架构或推理 kernel
limitations: 仅在合成 trace（非真实生产 trace）上评估，统计显著性有限（60 请求子集）
单 A100 GPU 评估，未验证多 GPU/disaggregated serving 场景
Tool 延迟通过 proportional scaling 模拟，非真实 tool 执行
Prompt splitting 效果取决于 prompt template 设计，不同框架的 tool-independent 比例可能差异大
10-15% 的改进幅度在高 QPS 下会缩小（被 engine 排队时间主导）

infra_impact.agent: 核心影响层——直接优化 agentic workload 的 FTR 延迟，为 LangChain/AutoGen 等框架提供 co-design 接口范式
infra_impact.kernel: 不需要自定义 kernel，所有优化在 scheduler/orchestrator 层面
infra_impact.cluster: 单 GPU 评估，co-design API 概念可扩展到多节点但未验证
infra_impact.framework: 为 vLLM 等 serving engine 增加 agentic workload 原生支持能力，semantic KV tagging 为 cache 管理开辟新方向

open_questions: 在 disaggregated P-D serving（prefill/decode 分离）架构下，partial prefill 的 KV cache 跨 GPU 传输如何处理？
更大模型（70B+）在 TP/PP 下 co-design API 的扩展和延迟特征如何？
streaming tool dispatch 在 tool call 依赖关系（sequential tools）场景下的效果如何？
semantic KV tagging 能否自动化学习最优驱逐策略，而非手动设定优先级？
真实生产 workload（非合成 trace）下改进幅度是否一致？

papers.title: Concur: Proactive Agent-Level Admission Control for Efficient Agentic Batch Inference
papers.core_contribution: Concur reframes GPU KV-cache as a shared finite resource and ports TCP AIMD congestion control to agent-level admission, using KV usage + hit-rate dual-signal feedback to eliminate middle-phase thrashing in agentic batch inference—delivering up to 4.09x throughput on Qwen3-32B and 1.90x on DeepSeek-V3 without engine modification.
papers.summary: Agentic batch inference (RL rollouts, data distillation, agent-as-judge) stresses GPU KV-cache in a sustained, cumulative manner because ReAct-style agents grow their context monotonically and pause asynchronously for tool calls. The authors identify and characterize 'middle-phase thrashing': a three-phase execution pattern (warmup -> thrashing -> cooldown) where KV usage stays near saturation but hit rate collapses, causing ~49% of end-to-end latency to be wasted on eviction-induced recomputation. Existing fixes fail: smarter eviction still targets idle-but-live prefixes; CPU offloading (HiCache) saturates PCIe under concurrency (can be 3x slower than baseline); request-level admission lacks visibility into agent-cumulative cache footprint and can make things worse (0.72x). Concur introduces a lightweight middleware between agent frameworks and the LLM serving engine (SGLang), exposing admit/pause/resume primitives on agent lifecycles. It ports TCP AIMD control with a conjunctive congestion signal U_t > U_high AND H_t < H_thresh, and sustains high throughput with fixed parameters (alpha=2, beta=0.5, U_low=0.2, U_high=0.5). Evaluation on H100 clusters shows 6/6 wins across Qwen3-32B and DeepSeek-V3 configurations; KV hit rate stays at 73-96% where SGLang collapses to 35%; the method beats both static admission and HiCache offloading consistently.
key_findings: Middle-phase thrashing is a distinct performance pathology from classical memory thrashing: memory stays full but hit rate collapses; dominates >90% of execution time and 49% of latency as recomputation overhead.
Request-level admission control can be WORSE than no control (0.72x on Qwen3-32B/TP8) because it lacks agent-cumulative cache visibility.
CPU offloading (HiCache) fails under concurrency due to PCIe contention: achieves 97% hit rate but up to 0.34x speed on DeepSeek-V3/B16.
A dual-signal conjunctive trigger (usage AND hit-rate) is the core engineering insight—usage alone misclassifies healthy high-load as congestion, causing 4-5x latency regression.
AIMD with TCP default parameters (alpha=2, beta=0.5) generalizes across models and workloads; only U_low needs careful calibration.
Static admission limits cannot match adaptive control because workload phase changes (1.5-2.9x gap between best static and Concur).
limitations: Offline batch only; no online/SLO (TTFT, TPOT) evaluation.
Assumes ReAct-style agents with step boundaries; streaming and multi-agent communication not addressed.
Hit-rate signal assumes workload with baseline shared prefixes; may fail on high-diversity workloads where hit rate is always low.
Not integrated with P-D disaggregation, speculative decoding, or multi-engine deployments.
Heterogeneous agent cohorts (mix of short and long agents) may suffer starvation under a single W_t window.
No open-source release announced; no comparison to concurrent agent-native systems (TokenCake, Continuum, Kairos, Autellix).

infra_impact.algorithm: Enables larger effective batch sizes for agentic RL rollouts, directly speeding up policy training; removes a throughput cliff that forced under-batching.
infra_impact.kernel: N/A — pure control plane; uses existing paged-attention / prefix-cache kernels unchanged, but lets them operate under a stable bounded footprint.
infra_impact.framework: Defines a new middleware layer (Agent-Level Controller) between agent frameworks and engines; generalizes request-level admission into agent-level admission with feedback control.
infra_impact.llm: No model architecture changes; validated on dense (Qwen3-32B) and MoE (DeepSeek-V3); models with larger per-token KV (e.g., non-MLA) benefit more.
infra_impact.agent: Core contribution layer—makes ReAct agents first-class scheduling units with pause/resume primitives naturally aligned to tool-call boundaries.
infra_impact.cluster: Indirect—eliminates thrashing-induced wasted GPU-hours, important for large-scale RL training clusters.
infra_impact.code: No open-source release disclosed in paper; implementation is a Python/C++ middleware layered on SGLang.

open_questions: How does Concur behave on heterogeneous agent cohorts (short + long agents mixed)? Does the single W_t cause starvation of long agents?
Can AIMD hyperparameters be learned online, or does the fixed (0.2, 0.5) setting truly generalize across diverse tasks (code, math, browsing)?
How should admission control interact with P-D disaggregation, speculative decoding, or multi-engine serving?
Under adversarial / divergent-prompt workloads where hit-rate never exceeds ~30%, does the controller get stuck in a permanent low-window regime?
How should multi-tenant QoS and priority agents integrate with agent-level admission control?

papers.title: Intelligence Degradation in Long-Context LLMs: Critical Threshold Determination via Natural Length Distribution Analysis
papers.core_contribution: Natural Length Distribution Analysis 评测方法学 + 5-method cross-validation, 在 Qwen2.5-7B 上量化 long-context cliff-like degradation, 临界点 Lc ≈ 43.2 % (≈ 55 296 tokens)，F1 从 0.556 跌到 0.302 (45.5 %)。
papers.summary: 本文针对长上下文 LLM 的 'intelligence degradation' 现象，提出三件事：(1) Natural Length Distribution Analysis —— 用样本原生 token 长度评测，避免 truncation/padding 引入的人工 artifact；(2) 5-method cross-validation (gradient/2nd-derivative/binned/percentile/sliding-window) 取中位数得到临界点估计，σ_methods = 1.2 %；(3) 把 cliff 解释成 RoPE position aliasing、attention dispersion、information bottleneck 三个机制取最小的 unified framework。在 1 000 样本混合数据集 (500 SQuAD + 500 NarrativeQA) 上测得 Qwen2.5-7B 的临界点是 max_position 的 43.2 %（约 55K tokens, 128K 模型），F1 从 stable region 的 0.565 骤降到 degraded region 的 0.278，p < 0.001、Cohen's d = 8.2。理论侧 RoPE 给出 49 % 上界，实测 43.2 %，差 6 pp 暗示 attention/info 机制是更紧的约束。论文给出的 *deployable rule of thumb* 是 'prompt 不要超过 advertised context 的 40 %'，对推理框架的 admission control / agent 上下文管理有直接指导意义。
key_findings: Qwen2.5-7B 在 r ≈ 43.2 % (≈ 55 296 tokens) 出现 cliff-like 智能衰退，F1 0.556 → 0.302 (45.5 %)；超过临界点后 F1 持续在 0.25–0.30 不恢复，p<0.001、Cohen's d=8.2
Natural-length 评测 σ = 1.2 %, 比 truncation 评测的 σ = 3.2 % 更稳定，证明截断/补零本身会引入显著噪声
RoPE 周期 P ≈ 62 832 tokens, 单独预测 L_RoPE ≈ 49 %, 实测 43.2 % 比它早 6 pp ⇒ attention dispersion / 信息瓶颈是更紧的约束 (Lc = min{L_RoPE, L_attn, L_info})
实用法则：把实际 prompt 控制在 advertised context 的 40 % 以内 (128K 模型 ≈ 51 200 tokens) 才能稳定在 plateau 上
Position encoding 决定 cliff 位置：standard RoPE 40-50 %、interpolated RoPE 60-70 %、ALiBi 70-80 % (Table 4)
Mixed dataset (SQuAD 5-10 % + NarrativeQA 20-95 %) 是 detector 工作的必要条件，单数据集会造成 survivor bias 或观察不到 cliff
limitations: 只测了 Qwen2.5-7B 和 reading comprehension，3B/32B、Llama/Mistral、其它任务未验证
信息瓶颈 η(L) 与 attention concentration C(A,L) 只给了 conceptual 公式，未真正测量内部 attention weights，机制分析停留在概念层
5 个 detector 中 4 个共享 3-stage filter，σ_methods = 1.2 % 的 method-independence 部分被高估；本质只有 2 类独立检测器 (gradient-family vs binned)
Figure 1 的 trend line 看起来更像 gradual decline + 局部 dip 而非干净 cliff；'45.5 % drop' 高度依赖 Definition 3 中 θ = 30 % 这个任意阈值
F1 作为唯一智能度量噪声大，未与 NIAH/RULER/LongBench 等细粒度长上下文 benchmark 对比
transition 区 (40-50 %) 实际样本不到 100 个，σ = 0.089 偏大，detector 选峰对采样噪声敏感

infra_impact.algorithm: 给 long-context post-training (long-context SFT, YaRN-scaling, ProLong-style upsample) 提供量化优化目标 —— 把 L_attention/L_info 推到 L_RoPE 之上
infra_impact.kernel: 暗示 sparse / sliding-window attention kernel (Longformer, StreamingLLM, dual-chunk attention) 在 > 40 % context 时有质量动机而不仅是性能动机
infra_impact.framework: 推理框架 (vLLM/SGLang/TRT-LLM) 的 scheduler / admission control 可以引入 'soft context budget = 40 % advertised' 上限；KV cache sizing 应按 stable region 而非 max_position 估算成本
infra_impact.llm: 给 LLM 模型卡里 max context 字段一个客观打折标尺，业界宣传的 128K/1M 应该附带 'stable context @ X % degradation' 二级指标
infra_impact.agent: agent 多轮历史 + tool output + 文档拼接很容易突破 40 %，本文给 agent context-management (compressor / summary memory / selective retention) 提供量化触发点
infra_impact.code: N/A — 论文不涉及框架/库 PR；但作者提供的 GitHub (charles-wang888/intelligence-degradation-in-llm) 是可以拿来跑其它模型的现成 evaluation harness

open_questions: 如果在 Qwen2.5-3B / Qwen2.5-32B 上跑，cliff 是稳定在 ~43 %、随参数量平移、还是与训练 data mix 强相关？
Llama-3.1 (NTK-aware RoPE)、Mistral (sliding-window)、Mamba (no positional bias) 的 cliff 形状如何？三瓶颈 framework 是否仍预测正确？
用 NIAH / RULER 复跑同一份 1000 prompts，detector 给出的 cliff 位置是否仍在 43 %？还是 metric 决定 cliff 形状？
如果做 long-context SFT (例如 ProLong / Qwen2.5-1M) 把 L_attn/L_info 抬高，cliff 是平移到 L_RoPE 还是直接消失？
Figure 1 看到的局部 dip 是 detector artifact (rise-in-range filter 选峰偏好) 还是真实 cliff？需要做更密的采样在 40–55 % 区间复测

papers.title: Latency-Optimal Load Balancing For Distributed MoE Inference
papers.core_contribution: Proposes a latency-optimal algorithm for expert replication and reallocation during distributed MoE inference that jointly minimizes load imbalance and data movement overhead via ILP formulation with a polynomial-time heuristic solver.
papers.summary: Expert parallelism for MoE inference creates load imbalance across devices, and prior dynamic rebalancing methods incur excessive data movement overhead. This paper formulates expert reallocation as an ILP jointly optimizing load balance and migration cost, then designs a fast greedy heuristic for runtime use. Results show up to 12.5% latency reduction over naive assignment and 2x more frequent rebalancing.
key_findings: Joint optimization of load imbalance and data movement overhead yields up to 12.5% MoE execution latency reduction
ILP formulation provides theoretical optimality baseline for expert placement decisions
Polynomial-time greedy heuristic closely approximates ILP solution while enabling 2x more frequent rebalancing
limitations: Only evaluated on scale-up networks, not scale-out cross-node settings
12.5% improvement is over naive assignment, not compared against latest dynamic methods
Workshop paper with limited experimental scope — no real MoE model end-to-end benchmarks

infra_impact.algorithm: ILP formulation provides principled optimization framework for expert placement
infra_impact.kernel: N/A
infra_impact.framework: Directly impacts inference serving frameworks implementing expert parallelism
infra_impact.llm: Benefits large MoE models by reducing inference latency without modifying architecture
infra_impact.agent: Indirectly reduces agent response times for MoE-based models
infra_impact.cluster: Designed for scale-up topologies; joint optimization approach relevant to cluster-level placement

open_questions: How to co-optimize expert placement with KV cache placement in disaggregated serving?
What is the optimal rebalancing frequency as a function of workload volatility?

papers.title: Microbenchmarking NVIDIA's Blackwell Architecture: An in-depth Architectural Analysis
papers.core_contribution: 首篇对 NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPU 的系统性 PTX 微基准测试分析，量化了 TMEM(-58%延迟)、tcgen05(2.9-11.6x低延迟)、硬件解压引擎(462 GB/s)和 FP4/FP6 新精度的微架构性能特征
papers.summary: GPU 架构迭代速度远超社区的深度理解。本文开发了首个 Blackwell B200 的 PTX 微基准测试套件，系统测量了三大架构创新：TMEM(cache-miss延迟420 cycles，降低58%)、第五代 Tensor Core(tcgen05.mma 延迟11.0-11.4 cycles，比 wgmma 降低2.9-11.6x)、硬件解压引擎(Bitcomp 462.4 GB/s)。端到端实测 B200 在 LLM 推理上比 H200 快 1.57-1.59x，训练快 1.56x 且能效提升 42%，FP64 科学计算快 1.92x。
key_findings: TMEM cache-miss 延迟 420 cycles，比 Hopper 全局内存延迟 1000 cycles 降低 58%
tcgen05.mma 单指令延迟 11.0-11.4 cycles 近乎恒定，证明 Blackwell 采用空间阵列设计而非时域流水线
FP32 accumulator 使 Tensor Core 吞吐减半（1929→965 TFLOPS），accumulator datapath 是瓶颈
FP4 达到 7702.5 TFLOPS (96.3% 峰值)，推理 perplexity 退化 +7.7-9.1%
硬件解压引擎呈现 decompression-throughput bounded 特征，输出吞吐稳定 160-220 GB/s
B200 在所有精度上达到 96-99% 理论峰值，Tensor Core 不是瓶颈，内存带宽才是
训练 1.56x 加速分解为 SM增量(1.09x) × CTA pairing(1.27x) × TMEM(1.26x)
limitations: 所有微基准在单 GPU 上测量，dual-die NV-HBI 通信开销未量化
CUDA 13.0 对 TMEM/CTA 支持是 preliminary 的，实际框架可达性能可能低于微基准
FP6 硬件支持存在但缺乏软件工具链
FP4 perplexity 退化是模型级平均值，per-layer 退化分布未分析
未与 AMD MI300X/MI355X 进行横向对比

infra_impact.algorithm: FP4 +8.2% perplexity退化要求per-layer precision selection；FP32 accumulator吞吐减半影响混合精度训练策略
infra_impact.kernel: TMEM最优tile从32×32变为64×64需要重新tune；tcgen05 warp-level执行改变kernel调度策略
infra_impact.framework: vLLM/TRT-LLM需适配tcgen05+TMEM；DE需框架层集成压缩模型加载；FP6缺乏软件工具链
infra_impact.llm: FP4使70B模型可单卡推理；MoE从量化获益更大(2.69x vs 2.50x)
infra_impact.agent: BS=1延迟12.3ms对实时agent有意义；P99/median=1.12-1.14使尾延迟更可控

open_questions: NV-HBI dual-die 通信对实际 workload 的 NUMA-like 效应如何量化？
TMEM 在 cache-hit 场景下的实际延迟分布是什么？
FP4 per-layer precision selection 的最优策略是什么？
tcgen05 在 FlashAttention kernel 中的实际加速比是多少？
CUDA 13.0 之后的版本是否能充分利用 TMEM 双缓冲？

papers.title: Eliminating Multi-GPU Performance Taxes: A Systems Approach to Efficient Distributed LLMs
papers.core_contribution: Identifies and quantifies 'performance taxes' in multi-GPU LLM systems (synchronization, serialization, memory management overhead), proposing systems-level solutions to eliminate them.

papers.title: HipKittens: Fast and Furious AMD Kernels
papers.core_contribution: First systematic C++ embedded tile-based programming framework for AMD GPUs, introducing 8-wave ping-pong scheduling, explicit register pinning, and chiplet-aware cache optimization that match or outperform hand-optimized assembly kernels.
papers.summary: AMD GPUs offer competitive peak compute and memory bandwidth but lack mature software support, with peak-performance kernels requiring raw assembly. HipKittens (HK) extends ThunderKittens' tile-based programming paradigm to AMD, demonstrating that tile abstractions generalize across vendors but their instantiation algorithms must be rethought. HK introduces 8-wave ping-pong scheduling (replacing wave specialization which underperforms on AMD due to static register allocation), developer-controlled register pinning (bypassing HIPCC limitations), heterogeneous swizzle strategies for AMD's non-compositional MFMA layouts, and a chiplet-aware XCD grid scheduling algorithm. Validated on MI325X/MI355X, HK competes with AMD's hand-optimized assembly on GEMM and attention forward, while outperforming all baselines by 1.2-2.5x on under-served workloads like GQA backward, d=64 attention, and memory-bound kernels.
key_findings: Wave specialization achieves only 80% of peak BF16 GEMM on AMD due to static register allocation
8-wave ping-pong schedule matches hand-optimized assembly performance with only 48 lines of hot loop code for FP8 GEMM
Explicit register pinning bypasses HIPCC to use AGPRs as MFMA inputs, improving attention backward by 20%
Chiplet-aware XCD scheduling achieves 19% speedup over naive row-major grid order
HK outperforms all AMD baselines on GQA backward by 1.8-2.5x
Triton compiler on AMD is consistently outperformed by 1.3-3.0x on GEMM and up to 2x on attention
limitations: Only validated on AMD CDNA3/CDNA4; no RDNA, Intel, or other architectures
No end-to-end inference/training throughput benchmarks — only kernel microbenchmarks
Chiplet scheduling parameters require manual tuning per problem size
Register pinning bypasses compiler safety checks — may be fragile across ROCm versions

infra_impact.algorithm: Enables rapid prototyping of new AI algorithms on AMD by providing familiar tile-based abstractions
infra_impact.kernel: Establishes first systematic set of AMD kernel optimization principles as foundation for future AMD kernel libraries
infra_impact.framework: HK kernels integrate into PyTorch via Python bindings, potentially replacing suboptimal SDPA/Triton backends
infra_impact.llm: Competitive GEMM and attention performance on MI355X means LLM training/inference can achieve near-peak hardware utilization
infra_impact.agent: Lower kernel latency on AMD could enable real-time agent inference
infra_impact.cluster: Chiplet-aware scheduling insights apply to multi-GPU AMD clusters

open_questions: Can the 8-wave ping-pong pattern generalize to non-AI workloads?
What is the end-to-end LLM serving throughput improvement when replacing framework kernels with HK?
How do HK kernels perform on MoE workloads with grouped/batched GEMMs?

papers.title: Optimizing Attention on GPUs by Exploiting GPU Architectural NUMA Effects
papers.core_contribution: 在 AMD MI300X 多 chiplet 架构上提出 Swizzled Head-first Mapping，将同一 attention head 的 workgroups 映射到同一 XCD 以最大化 L2 cache 复用，实现 FA2 最高 50% 性能提升和 80-97% L2 cache hit rate。
papers.summary: 现代 GPU 从单 die 向多 chiplet 架构演进引入 NUMA 效应。MI300X 的 8 XCD 各有独立 4MB L2 cache。FA2 默认 round-robin 调度将同一 head 的 workgroups 分散到不同 XCD，导致 K/V 数据无法在 L2 中复用。Swizzled Head-first Mapping 通过 ~15 行 swizzle 逻辑将同一 head 的所有 blocks 限制在同一 XCD，使 K/V 只需加载一次到该 XCD 的 L2。MHA H=128/128K 下性能提升 50%，L2 hit 从 ~1% 到 97%。
key_findings: MI300X 上 FA2 默认调度导致 L2 cache hit rate 在 H=128/N=128K 下暴跌至 ~1%
Swizzled Head-first 将 L2 hit rate 提升到 80-97%，MHA 最高 50% 性能提升
MHA 收益 >> GQA 收益（GQA 因 KV groups=XCDs 天然 cache 友好）
Forward 50% speedup，backward 仅 ~10%（受其他瓶颈限制）
DeepSeek-V3 prefill (MHA H=128) 是最佳 case study
极小代码改动 (~15 行 Triton swizzle)，零 compute overhead
limitations: 仅在 AMD MI300X 验证，不适用于 NVIDIA Blackwell (硬件 coherent)
Backward pass 收益有限 (~10%)
Head 数 < XCD 数时方法退化
未评估 decode 阶段和 PagedAttention/MLA 兼容性

infra_impact.algorithm: ACC-to-XCD mapping 原则可泛化到任何 chiplet GPU tiled kernel
infra_impact.kernel: ~15 行 Triton swizzle 可直接集成到 AITER 和 Triton FA
