infra_impact.framework: MI300X 上 vLLM/SGLang prefill attention 可直接加速
infra_impact.llm: MHA 高 head 数模型（DeepSeek-V3）收益最大
infra_impact.agent: Prefill 加速→TTFT 降低→改善交互延迟

open_questions: MI350X (CDNA4) 上 L2 cache 拓扑变化后方法是否仍适用?
NVIDIA Rubin Ultra (4 die) 是否需要类似的软件 NUMA 优化?
Decode 阶段（per-request 不同 K/V）如何适配?
与 PagedAttention、MLA 等 attention 变体的兼容性?

papers.title: Scaling Graph Chain-of-Thought Reasoning: A Multi-Agent Framework with Efficient LLM Serving
papers.core_contribution: GLM is the first multi-agent Graph-CoT system co-designed with a Graph-CoT-aware LLM serving stack (vertex-centric KV reuse + four-tier priority eviction + RetrieveNode/decode pipeline), reducing token cost by 95.7%, latency by 90.3%, and boosting throughput 15.1x while lifting Graph-CoT accuracy by up to 38%.
papers.summary: Graph-CoT augments LLMs with iterative graph retrieval for structured multi-hop reasoning, but existing single-agent pipelines pay heavy token/latency costs (40k+ tokens, $3+ per query, 30s+ latency) while accuracy stalls below the 0.50 reliability threshold due to the lost-in-the-middle effect; serving-side, vLLM prefix caching only hits ~61%, LRU evicts to-be-reused graph facts, and vector retrieval is fully serialized. GLM jointly redesigns both layers: (1) decomposes the monolithic agent into a Classification Agent (deterministic vs. non-deterministic routing), a Reasoning Agent that operates on an incrementally-built notebook, an Action Agent that generates executable Python snippets (collapsing k1 LLM rounds into k2+1), and a Graph-RAG Retriever exposing {RetrieveNode, NodeInfo, NodeFeature, NodeDegree, neighbourCheck}; (2) introduces a Graph-CoT-aware serving stack with a vertex-centric KV reuse unit (a node plus its top-k neighbours forms a stable, nameable cache key), a four-tier priority eviction policy (shared prompt prefix P-I permanently kept, active notebook P-II, archived notebook P-III, transient missing-info P-IV, LRU within each tier), and a pipelined execution strategy that streaming-detects the RetrieveNode line in Action-Agent output to trigger asynchronous retrieval overlapping with decode, plus an LRU text->nodeID cache. On GRBench (1040 QA across 5 domains), GLM improves R-L from 0.31-0.46 to 0.55-0.77 (peak +38%), cuts per-query cost from $1.7-$3.6 to $0.1-$0.2, reduces latency from 11.3-38.6s to 2.8-5.9s, and achieves 6.8-9.1 QPS vs. 0.6-2.2 QPS (up to 15.1x).
key_findings: Multi-agent decomposition beats longer CoT: splitting classify/reason/act eliminates lost-in-the-middle, driving unexpected-agent-output errors from 41% to 2% and step-limit errors from 37% to 4%.
Code-generation Action Agent collapses k1 LLM rounds into k2+1 (avg LLM calls per query: 9-14 -> 2-3; per-call tokens: 1875-4483 -> 769-991).
Vertex chunk (node + top-k neighbours) as the KV reuse unit is the single biggest lever: +17.7% hit rate and +41.6% throughput from this alone.

LRU is a reverse signal in Graph-CoT: the most-recently-generated A-Agent suffix is the least reusable; semantic priority (shared-prefix > notebook > archived-notebook > transient) beats vLLM single-queue LRU by +14.3% throughput / -15.1% miss.
RetrieveNode almost always appears as the first line of Action-Agent snippets, enabling deterministic (not speculative) overlap with decode; pipeline alone cuts latency by 47.8%.
Accuracy gains concentrate on information-aggregation multi-hop tasks (E-commerce R-L 0.39->0.77, Healthcare 0.33->0.62) rather than single-fact lookups (Legal 0.46->0.63).
Code generation is not free lunch: code-execution errors jump from 2% to 21%, requiring a timeout + self-correction fault-tolerance loop.
limitations: Accuracy ceiling bounded by backbone LLM capacity (Qwen3-235B-A22B); requires distillation/LoRA/structure-aware prompting to decouple.
Retrieval remains dominant bottleneck on very large graphs (e.g. Legal 84M nodes) where retrieval exceeds decode window, weakening pipeline gains.
Code-execution error becomes the new dominant failure mode (48% of errors); mitigation via self-correction is heuristic, no systematic verifier.
Priority eviction and streaming RetrieveNode hook depend on non-standard vLLM v0.8.5 APIs; porting to SGLang/TRT-LLM requires rework.
C-Agent deterministic/non-deterministic classifier not quantified (no FP/FN reported); distribution shift could silently degrade.
Vertex chunk top-k neighbour truncation (by degree) may drop low-degree but query-relevant neighbours.
Notebook has no compression policy; very long multi-hop sessions may inflate Priority-II cache.

infra_impact.algorithm: Provides a reusable classify/reason/act decomposition recipe for agentic RAG; applicable to KGQA, structured information extraction, and task planning; can serve as an environment template for agentic RL (GRPO-style).
infra_impact.kernel: N/A — no new kernels; all wins come from scheduler/cache management on top of vLLM paged attention.
infra_impact.framework: Major contribution: introduces semantic priority KV eviction (replacing pure LRU) and retrieval-aware deterministic pipelining as generalizable primitives for multi-agent serving; direct relevance to vLLM / SGLang prefix-caching policy and multi-agent session APIs.
infra_impact.llm: No model modifications; multi-agent decomposition alleviates long-context lost-in-the-middle pressure, offering an alternative to raw context-window scaling.
infra_impact.agent: Formalizes the idea that agent boundaries equal KV-priority boundaries; turns multi-agent from prompt engineering into serving-system primitives; relevant to LangGraph / AutoGen / MetaGPT style orchestrators.
infra_impact.cluster: N/A — single-node (8x A800 NVLink); no networking or cross-node innovations.
infra_impact.code: ~3K Python + vLLM v0.8.5 fork; priority-eviction + streaming hook patches could become an upstream RFC to vLLM/SGLang for multi-agent session support.

open_questions: Does the RetrieveNode-first-line assumption hold across different backbones and coding styles?
Can priority tiers be learned online from runtime hit/miss logs rather than hand-engineered?
How does the approach scale when distilled Action Agents (7B/13B) replace the 235B backbone while keeping code-execution reliable?
Can vertex chunk granularity be made adaptive per query (degree vs. semantic relevance vs. PageRank)?
Can the approach integrate with SGLang RadixAttention or Mooncake-style multi-tier global KV stores?

papers.title: Retrieval Quality at Context Limit
papers.core_contribution: Empirically demonstrates that the 'Lost in the Middle' U-shaped accuracy curve has been completely eliminated for single-needle factoid Q&A in Gemini 2.5 Flash up to its full ~1.05M-token context window — 26/26 questions correct at every tested fill ratio from 13% to 92% of the model's max context.
papers.summary: Liu et al. (2023) established that early long-context LLMs (GPT-3.5, Llama-2) followed a U-shaped accuracy curve when retrieving facts from long documents — high at the start and end, sharply degraded in the middle — a phenomenon dubbed 'Lost in the Middle' (LITM) attributed to causal attention masks and Rotary-style positional encodings. This paper revisits LITM with Gemini 2.5 Flash (1M+ token window) and a clean controlled experiment. The author injects 20 non-canon dialogue snippets (yielding 26 Q&A pairs) at evenly spaced positions throughout the Friends TV-show transcript (924k words ≈ >1M Gemini tokens), then sweeps the trimmed transcript size from 10% to 80% of the full corpus and submits a single batched prompt containing context plus all 26 questions. The 80% step triggers an HTTP 400 ('input token count (1,105,498) exceeds 1,048,576'), pinning the true API context cap; every smaller ratio (up to 92% of the context window) returns 26/26 correct answers. Ablation confirms answers come from the injected needles, not training memory of Friends. The author hypothesises three non-mutually-exclusive causes — positional encoding upgrades (e.g. ALiBi-style biases), training curricula that explicitly stress NIAH retrieval, and undisclosed architectural changes — but cannot disentangle them because Gemini 2.5 weights are closed. The paper is honest about scope: only single-needle factoid retrieval is tested; multi-needle, multi-hop, multimodal, and adversarial regimes remain difficult and are flagged as open future work. The result is operationally important: it gives downstream RAG/agent designers a citable reason to skip retrieval for simple lookup queries at 1M context, while preserving the case for retrieval in harder regimes.
key_findings: Gemini 2.5 Flash achieves 100% accuracy on 26 single-needle factoid questions at every tested context-fill ratio from 0.13× to 0.92× of its max context window — no Lost-in-the-Middle effect.
True API context limit is exactly 1,048,576 tokens (2^20); verified by the precise HTTP 400 message at 80% of the Friends transcript (1,105,498 tokens).
Empirical word-to-token ratio (60–80 English words per 100 Gemini tokens) is consistent — 70% of corpus = 647k words ≈ 0.97M tokens fits, 80% does not.
Ablation control confirms answers come from the injected needles, not from latent training data about Friends — the same questions asked without injection elicit 'unknown flavor' responses.

The paper does NOT falsify LITM in general — only LITM-on-single-needle for one specific frontier model. Multi-needle (LangChain 2024), multi-hop, multimodal (Vaidya 2025), and HELMET still report degradation.

Cost-of-experiment is the implicit secondary finding: Claude Sonnet 4 and GPT 4.1 were dropped because lower API tiers cap input length and high-tier access exceeds the academic budget — a real-world barrier to reproducible long-context evaluation.
limitations: Single-needle only — every conclusion evaporates at multi-needle, multi-hop, distractor-laden, or contradictory-fact regimes (paper acknowledges).
No paraphrased / adversarial / ambiguous queries — every question is a direct lookup of the injected fact; lexical robustness untested.
Single model — Claude Sonnet 4 and GPT 4.1 excluded due to API cost; cannot tell whether LITM disappears industry-wide or is Gemini-specific.
Single corpus / single domain — Friends conversational English only; no code, math, multilingual, or structured data.
No statistical noise model — N=26 with a 100% point estimate has 95% CI of roughly [87%, 100%]; a true 90% accuracy is statistically consistent.
No mechanistic test of the proposed hypotheses (ALiBi vs training curriculum vs architectural change); the experiment cannot distinguish them because the model is closed-weight.
Position of questions not varied — all 26 questions sit at the end of the prompt (recency-bias zone); a stronger LITM stress test would put questions in the middle.
No reproducibility artefact released — no code, no full prompt YAML, no judging script.

infra_impact.algorithm: Suggests training curricula that explicitly stress long-context NIAH retrieval may be sufficient to eliminate LITM at single-needle granularity. Implies post-training synthetic 'needle' data should remain a default ingredient in long-context model recipes.
infra_impact.kernel: Largely orthogonal — but reinforces that long-context kernels (FlashAttention, Ring/Tree Attention, sliding-window variants) are now consistently expected to deliver flat accuracy across positions, raising the kernel correctness bar (no quiet under-performance for middle positions).
infra_impact.framework: Soft signal for RAG framework designers that single-needle 'stuff everything in context' is now viable at 1M tokens — chunking + retrieval is no longer mandatory for simple lookup queries. Trade-off shifts from accuracy to prefill cost.
infra_impact.llm: Validates that frontier 1M-context LLMs have closed the basic LITM gap; future model-architecture work should target multi-needle, multi-hop, and multimodal retrieval where degradation persists. The paper itself does not propose new architecture.
infra_impact.agent: Agents that ingest large transcripts/codebases can now trust single-fact lookups across the full window, simplifying memory designs that previously assumed positional decay. Caveat: nothing here justifies skipping retrieval for multi-document or multi-hop reasoning agents.
infra_impact.code: N/A — no source code, prompts, or judging artefacts released; reproducibility depends on closed-API access and re-implementation from the methods description.

open_questions: Where does single-needle accuracy actually break? At 2M, 4M, 10M tokens — if the model supported it — does the curve eventually bend down?
Does the disappearance of LITM transfer to multi-needle, multi-hop, and multimodal regimes as those models also scale, or is single-needle a special case?

What is the actual mechanism — position encoding, training curriculum, or architecture? A controlled ablation (same architecture trained with vs without NIAH curriculum) would settle it but requires a frontier lab.
Cost economics: at 1M-token prefill, when does 'stuff everything in context' actually beat RAG + small model on $/query for single-fact lookup?
Does enabling Gemini 2.5 Flash 'thinking' mode change the result — could reveal whether reasoning models compound retrieval accuracy or introduce new failure modes.

papers.title: fabric-lib: RDMA Point-to-Point Communication for LLM Systems
papers.core_contribution: A user-space RDMA library optimized for LLM inference workloads, providing zero-copy point-to-point transfers that outperform NCCL by up to 3.6x for KV-cache and tensor transfers in disaggregated serving.

papers.title: LongCat-Video Technical Report
papers.core_contribution: A unified 13.6B video generation model that natively supports long video generation through video-continuation pretraining, with multi-reward GRPO post-training for flow matching and efficient coarse-to-fine inference with 3D block sparse attention achieving 12x speedup.
papers.summary: LongCat-Video is a 13.6B parameter Diffusion Transformer from Meituan that unifies T2V, I2V, and Video-Continuation tasks by treating all as conditional video continuation. It introduces Block Causal Attention with KV caching, improved GRPO for flow matching (interpreting GRPO as stochastic noise search), multi-reward training (VQ+MQ+TA) that prevents reward hacking, and a coarse-to-fine inference pipeline combined with 3D block sparse attention (93.75% sparsity) for 12.3x speedup. On VBench 2.0 it scores 62.11% (1st open-source) and leads all models in Commonsense (70.94%).
key_findings: 12.3x inference speedup through LCM distillation + coarse-to-fine + block sparse attention on a single H800 GPU
Multi-reward GRPO prevents reward hacking that single-reward training exhibits
3D Block Sparse Attention achieves near-lossless quality at 93.75% sparsity
70.94% on VBench 2.0 Commonsense — best across all models including Veo3
GRPO as stochastic noise search theoretical insight provides foundation for training techniques
Coarse-to-fine not only accelerates but improves quality over native 720p generation
limitations: I2V overall quality lags behind Seedance 1.0, Hailuo-02, and Wan2.2
RLHF training only uses T2V tasks — no task-specific rewards for I2V or Video-Continuation
No model quantization explored despite 13.6B parameter size

papers.title: UniVideo: Unified Understanding, Generation, and Editing for Videos
papers.core_contribution: UniVideo proposes a dual-stream architecture (frozen MLLM + MMDiT) that unifies video understanding, generation, and editing under a single multimodal instruction paradigm, using a 3-stage training strategy and demonstrating zero-shot generalization to unseen tasks and task compositions.
papers.summary: UniVideo combines a frozen MLLM (Qwen2.5VL-7B) for instruction understanding with an MMDiT (HunyuanVideo-13B) for video generation, connected through MLP connector using self-attention alignment. A 3-stage training strategy enables T2V, I2V, in-context video generation, and editing from a single model. It achieves 0.88 subject consistency in in-context generation (vs. 0.68 for Kling1.6) and demonstrates zero-shot generalization from image editing to free-form video editing.
key_findings: MMDiT self-attention alignment is superior to cross-attention for connecting MLLM with diffusion generator
Multi-task training significantly improves over single-task baselines
Dual-branch visual input is critical — removing visual input to MMDiT causes SC to drop from 0.78 to 0.18
Zero-shot generalization from image editing to video editing emerges from unified training
3D positional embeddings outperform MRoPE for distinguishing condition vs. noisy latents
UniVideo is the only model supporting understanding + generation + editing + in-context generation simultaneously
limitations: Sometimes over-edits unrelated regions
Struggles to fully preserve motion of original videos due to backbone limitations
Free-form video editing success rate lower than image editing
Post-trained assembled system rather than native end-to-end multimodal model

papers.title: Kimi Linear: An Expressive, Efficient Attention Architecture
papers.core_contribution: 首个在公平对比下全面超越 Full Attention 的混合线性注意力架构。核心 KDA (Kimi Delta Attention) 把 Gated DeltaNet 的 scalar gate 替换为 per-channel diagonal gate Diag(α_t)；通过 DPLR 约束 (a=b=k) 让 chunkwise kernel 比通用 DPLR 快 ~2x；层级混合 3 KDA + 1 MLA (NoPE), 实测 3:1 最优。
papers.summary: Kimi Linear 解决线性注意力多年的'质量-效率'两难。KDA 递推 S_t = (I − β_t k_t k_tᵀ) Diag(α_t) S_{t-1} + β_t k_t v_tᵀ, 每 channel 独立遗忘率, 比 Mamba2 scalar gate 表达力强、比 GLA full gate 高效。架构: 27L 总, 实际 20 KDA + 7 MLA (≈ 2.86:1, 论文设计 3:1)。MoE 48B/3B: 256 routed + 1 shared top-8, expert hidden 1024。MLA 用 NoPE (mla_use_nope=True) 不施加 RoPE, 位置/recency 完全靠 KDA gates + ShortConv。每 KDA 块: q/k/v 各一路 ShortConv k=4 + Swish, L2 norm on q/k, fused_kda_gate (g = -exp(A_log)·softplus(α + dt_bias)), 核心递推 (state 128×128 per head), FusedRMSNormGated sigmoid 输出, o_proj。KV cache: KDA 层无 per-token KV (固定 128×128 state + 3-step conv state), MLA 层 576 floats/token; 整体 ~75% KV 减少 vs 纯 MLA。1M context 通过 NoPE + KDA gates 而非 YaRN。训练: pretrain 1.4T (fair) + 5.7T (release) @ MuonClip + WSD lr=1.1e-3 batch=32M; SFT 两段 (IF → reasoning); RL 用 K1.5 算法 + PTX loss + truncated importance sampling + dynamic KL.
key_findings: KDA 公式: S_t = (I − β_t k_t k_tᵀ) Diag(α_t) S_{t-1} + β_t k_t v_tᵀ
Diagonal gate 比 scalar (Mamba2) 表达力强, 比 full (GLA) 高效
Hybrid 3:1 最优 (消融): 0:1 pure MLA 最差, 15:1 太疏退化
Released: 27L = 20 KDA + 7 MLA (≈ 2.86:1, 27 不被 4 整除)
NoPE on MLA > RoPE on MLA (Table 5), KDA 隐式提供 recency 已足够
Output gate sigmoid > Swish > none
ShortConv (k=4 causal) on q/k/v 必需 (消融)
L2 norm on q,k per-head per-channel 是数值稳定性关键
DPLR 约束 a=b=k 让 chunkwise 跳过部分 secondary chunk matmul → ~2x kernel 加速
Per-head state 128×128 = 16K floats (32 heads → 524K floats / KDA layer)
1M context: NoPE + KDA gates, no YaRN needed
Long context decode throughput +75% (paper Fig. 2)
KV memory ~75% reduction vs pure MLA stack
MoE: 256 routed top-8 + 1 shared, expert hidden 1024 (vs K2.5: 384/2048)
limitations: 纯线性注意力在精确复制和细粒度选择上仍有局限，需要混合设计
Scaling law优势适度(~1.16x)，可能受超参数敏感
5.7T模型的48B total vs Moonlight 16B total使直接对比不完全公平
Fine-grained decay引入数值精度问题，需secondary chunking

papers.title: AMD MI300X GPU Performance Analysis
papers.core_contribution: 首个系统性、多维度的 AMD MI300X vs NVIDIA GPU 性能基准测试，通过频率-效率分离实验精确量化了软件栈效率（80-85%）和频率降低（~42%）各自对性能差距的贡献。
papers.summary: 本文对 AMD MI300X GPU 在 LLM 推理场景下的三个关键性能维度（compute、memory、communication）进行了系统性基准测试，并与 NVIDIA H100/H200/B200 进行对比。MI300X 仅达到理论峰值 FLOPs 的 45%（vs NVIDIA 93%），memory bandwidth 达 81%（4.3 TB/s），通信带宽达 70%。端到端 Llama 70B FP8 推理中仅达 H100 的 37-66%。频率分离实验表明软件效率 80-85%，频率降至 boost clock 的 52-58%，两者共同解释了性能差距。结论：AMD 硬件规格已具竞争力，软件栈成熟度是核心瓶颈。
key_findings: MI300X compute 利用率仅 45%（vs NVIDIA 93%），频率降低（~42%降频）和软件效率（80-85%）各贡献约一半

papers.title: Justitia: Fair and Efficient Scheduling of Task-parallel LLM Agents with Selective Pampering
papers.core_contribution: Ports 1990s network WFQ (virtual-time fair queuing over GPS) to LLM application-level scheduling, using a memory-centric KV token-time cost (c = pd + d^2/2) and per-app TF-IDF+MLP demand prediction, achieving 57.5% average JCT reduction vs VTC with a provable worst-case delay bound of 2c_max + C_max/M.
papers.summary: Task-parallel LLM agents (MapReduce summarization, Self-Consistency, Fact Verification, ReAct, etc.) are served on shared GPU clusters where users care about end-to-end application completion time rather than individual inferences. Existing schedulers fail: vLLM/Parrot FCFS suffers head-of-line blocking, VTC/FairServe enforce short-term fair sharing that slows every app by restricting it to its 1/N share, and pure SRJF starves long apps. Justitia argues users want long-term (finish-time) fairness, not instantaneous equal-share, and therefore trades short-term fairness for serving apps sequentially in their GPS completion order (saturated serving) — each prioritized app can use all KV cache to finish fast, and the fair-completion-order prevents any app from being delayed versus the idealized fair scheduler. To operationalize this, Justitia models serving cost as KV token-time c = pd + d^2/2 (matching vLLM's actual KV-capacity bottleneck, with cost quadratic in decode length), predicts each app's total cost at arrival via a per-application 4-layer MLP over TF-IDF-vectorized prompts (2.16ms/53% error, vs Distillbert's 55.7ms/452%), and borrows WFQ's virtual time V(t) with dV/dt = M/N_t so the virtual finish time F_j = V(a_j) + C_j is computed once and never refreshed; scheduling is O(log N_t) min-heap. A formal proof bounds worst-case delay by 2c_max + C_max/M. Implemented atop vLLM and evaluated on LLaMA-7B (A100) / LLaMA-13B (4xV100) with 300 mixed agents on Mooncake arrival traces, Justitia reduces average JCT by 57.5% vs VTC and 61.1% vs Parrot, with 92% of apps finishing no later than under VTC (worst ratio 1.26) and near-SJF efficiency without SRJF's starvation problem.
key_findings: Memory is the true bottleneck for vLLM-like frameworks: KV token-time cost c = pd + d^2/2 (with quadratic dependence on decode length) is fundamentally better than VTC's linear compute-centric p+2d — ablation shows 42.3% JCT degradation when reverted.

Long-term (finish-time) fairness is the right fairness notion for LLM applications: giving up instantaneous equal-share enables saturated serving to achieve near-SJF efficiency while still guaranteeing no app is delayed versus the idealized fair scheduler.

Per-application small MLPs beat a single large Distillbert for cost prediction on every axis: 53% vs 452% error, 2.16ms vs 55.7ms inference, ~1min vs ~2h training — because different LLM applications have heterogeneous cost distributions.

Network WFQ (Parekh-Gallager 1993) transfers cleanly to LLM serving: treating KV-blocks as the divisible resource and LLM inferences as non-preemptable packets, virtual-time fair queuing gives O(log N_t) scheduling with no refresh on app arrival.
Formal worst-case delay bound f_j - GPS_f_j <= 2c_max + C_max/M; empirically tight because Mooncake traces show single requests average only 3.20% of total KV cache on LLaMA-3.3 70B.
Justitia avoids SRJF starvation: in elephant+mice micro-benchmark the elephant JCT stays bounded under Justitia while growing linearly with mice count under SRJF.
End-to-end: 57.5% average JCT reduction vs VTC (61.1% vs Parrot) across 300 mixed agents on A100/LLaMA-7B; scheduling overhead under 10ms even at 100 apps/min.
limitations: Only evaluated on single-node (A100) and small TP multi-GPU (4xV100); multi-node distributed deployment with global virtual-time synchronization is not designed or measured.
Requires per-application-type profiling data (100 samples per app); no cold-start strategy for newly-defined agent workflows (e.g., user-supplied LangChain chains) is provided.
KV token-time cost formula ignores prefix caching / RadixAttention; would over-estimate cost for apps with shared system prompts, making direct deployment on SGLang suboptimal without modification.
Does not support dynamic-fanout applications (Tree-of-Thoughts, MCTS) whose branching cost is unknown at arrival time.
Non-preemptive: a long in-flight decode cannot be interrupted by a high-priority arriving app until it finishes; Mooncake's 3.20% KV-per-request assumption is required for the tight delay bound.
Fairness metric (finish-time fair ratio) is defined relative to VTC, making it somewhat self-referential; absolute max-min fairness is not reported.
Does not integrate with disaggregated prefill/decode (DistServe) architectures where prefill and decode have separate KV lifetimes.

infra_impact.algorithm: Not directly about training algorithms, but inspires RL rollout / best-of-N sampling scheduling where aggregate KV cost can be used for early-stopping or reward shaping.
infra_impact.kernel: N/A — no kernel changes; operates purely in the CPU-side scheduler layer above vLLM's paged attention.
infra_impact.framework: Direct contribution: a drop-in application-level scheduler replacing vLLM's FCFS default, with memory-centric cost modeling and virtual-time fair queuing; forms a layered design point with Parrot (semantic variable), VTC (short-term fair), and SGLang's Radix-tree scheduler.
infra_impact.llm: Model-agnostic; assumes standard Transformer + KV cache. MLA/absorb-style architectures would need the KV token-time formula adjusted.
infra_impact.agent: Strongly relevant — specifically designed for agentic workloads (MapReduce, Self-Consistency, Fact Verification, ReAct). Provides finish-time fairness guarantee needed for multi-tenant agent hosting.
infra_impact.cluster: Gives a template for multi-tenant GPU-cluster scheduling combining finish-time fairness with SJF-like efficiency; complementary to Pollux/Sia goodput-optimized ML-cluster schedulers.
infra_impact.code: Implemented atop vLLM as a scheduler replacement; integration point is vLLM's Scheduler._schedule_default; ~few hundred LOC estimated.

open_questions: How to modify KV token-time cost for prefix-caching frameworks (SGLang RadixAttention) where shared prompts inflate the naive c = pd + d^2/2?
How to extend Justitia to disaggregated prefill/decode (DistServe / Mooncake) where prefill and decode have different KV lifetimes?
Can the framework handle cold-start applications (no profiling data) via online learning with bounded regret?
How to synchronize global virtual time V(t) across a distributed multi-node cluster without destroying the O(log N_t) complexity?
Does the constant delay bound still hold when adding SLO-aware weights (weighted WFQ) for paid-tier users?
How does dynamic-fanout (Tree-of-Thoughts, MCTS) reasoning — where branch count is runtime-determined — interact with the once-compute-never-refresh F_j design?

papers.title: SLA: Beyond Sparsity in Diffusion Transformers via Fine-Tunable Sparse-Linear Attention
papers.core_contribution: SLA fuses sparse and linear attention into a single GPU kernel by exploiting the natural decomposition of attention weights into high-rank sparse and low-rank dense components, achieving 95% attention sparsity and 13.7x kernel speedup without quality loss.
papers.summary: Attention computation is the dominant bottleneck in Diffusion Transformer (DiT) models for video generation. SLA addresses this by discovering that attention weights naturally decompose into a small high-rank component (<10%) and a large extremely low-rank component (>90%). It classifies attention weight blocks into critical (O(N²) FlashAttention), marginal (O(N) linear attention), and negligible (skipped), fusing all three paths into a single GPU kernel with both forward and backward passes. On Wan2.1-1.3B, SLA achieves 95% sparsity, 13.7x attention kernel speedup, and 2.2x end-to-end video generation speedup on RTX5090, while matching full attention quality with only 2000 fine-tuning steps.
key_findings: Attention weights in DiT decompose into high-rank sparse (<10%) and low-rank dense (>90%) components
SLA achieves 95% attention sparsity on Wan2.1-1.3B with no quality degradation
Fused GPU kernel delivers 13.7x forward attention speedup and 2.2x end-to-end speedup on RTX5090
Linear attention alone completely fails on video diffusion (VA: 0.042 vs 76.78)
Only 2000 fine-tuning steps needed — less than 0.1% of pretraining cost
Softmax outperforms ELU+1 and Hedgehog as the feature map in the linear attention component
limitations: Only evaluated on relatively small models (Wan2.1-1.3B, LightningDiT-1.0B)
Only tested on RTX5090; performance on other GPUs not reported
Requires fine-tuning — not a training-free drop-in replacement
Theoretical guarantees on when the sparse-lowrank decomposition holds are lacking

infra_impact.algorithm: Establishes the sparse-linear decomposition paradigm for efficient attention
infra_impact.kernel: Introduces novel fused kernel pattern (sparse + linear in one pass) with three optimization strategies
infra_impact.framework: Enables practical DiT video generation deployment by reducing attention cost

papers.title: Qwen3-Omni Technical Report
papers.core_contribution: 将 omni 推理拆为六段专门化流水线 (AuT → ViT → Thinker MoE → Talker MoE+Shared → Code Predictor → Code2Wav)，每段架构 (layers/d_model/heads/kv_heads/experts) 独立定制，总预算 30B-A3B 下做到 RTF<1、首包 234ms，并首次证明全模态无退化。
papers.summary: Qwen3-Omni 的核心在于 Thinker-Talker 架构的进一步精细拆分：六个子模块各自针对角色专门化——(1) AuT 音频编码器 32L/d=1280/20×64heads/8× 下采样到 12.5Hz；(2) SigLIP2 ViT 27L/d=1152/16×72heads + DeepStack 从层 8/16/24 取中间特征；(3) Thinker 28L/d=2048, GQA 28Q/4KV/head_dim=128, QK-Norm, 128E top-8 expert_dim=768；(4) Talker 20L/d=1024, GQA 16Q/2KV, 128E top-6 + Shared Expert (hidden=768) + per-token sigmoid gate；(5) Code Predictor 5L dense GQA 16Q/8KV + 15 套独立 LM head/embedding + 15 次 AR 迭代预测 residual codebooks；(6) Code2Wav 因果 ConvNet, left_context=25, chunk_size=10, 24kHz 流式输出。每个维度选择都有明确设计初衷：head_dim 统一 128 是 flash-attn 最优；Thinker GQA 7:1 保守、Talker 8:1 激进是因为 context 长度不同；Talker 的 shared expert+gate 是为了在 padding/silence token 省 FLOPs；Code Predictor 用独立 head 是因为不同 codebook 层分布差异大。训练 2T tokens，post-training 含 S2W Distillation + GSPO。Text/audio/vision 全面达到或超越同规模单模态模型。
key_findings: 六段专门化：AuT+ViT+Thinker+Talker+Code Predictor+Code2Wav，每段独立定制 (layers/d/heads/experts)
Thinker GQA 28Q/4KV + head_dim=128 → KV cache 仅 1024/token (vs dense MHA 7168)
Talker 独创 MoE+Shared Expert 组合：routed 128E top-6 (hidden=384) + shared (hidden=768) + per-token sigmoid gate
Code Predictor 是 5 层 dense 跑 15 次 AR 迭代，15 套独立 LM head/embedding 预测 RVQ 1..15 层
Vision DeepStack：从 ViT 层 8/16/24 取中间特征作为额外 token 喂给 Thinker
AuT 2× Conv1D + Temporal Merge = 8× 下采样 100Hz→12.5Hz 对齐 codec rate
Code2Wav 因果 ConvNet 替代 DiT，首包延迟 640ms+ → 3ms
首次证明全模态无退化：Omni-Base 在 text/vision 上 >= 单模态 base
36 audio benchmarks 中 32 开源 SOTA、22 总体 SOTA
MoE 免疫效应：1→6 并发 Thinker TPS 仅降 29% (75→53)
limitations: 长视频理解受限于位置外推能力和 context length
Thinking 模式在 ASR/music 等感知任务上不如 Instruct
语音生成仅支持 10 种语言（vs 119 种文本语言）
实验主要在 Qwen 系列内部对比
30B MoE 仍需较高端 GPU 部署

papers.title: Batch Query Processing and Optimization for Agentic Workflows
papers.core_contribution: Halo is the first system to bring database-style batch query optimization into agentic LLM workflows: it compiles N workflow DAGs into a consolidated graph and uses an epoch-based DP solver (with topological frontier pruning) over a state-aware cost model (T_prep + T_model + T_infer) to jointly decide LLM-node placement, batching, and CPU-tool coordination, while the runtime Processor adds Request Coalescing, wavefront, and opportunistic execution.
papers.summary: Existing LLM serving engines (vLLM, SGLang) optimize each request in isolation and multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph, AgentScope) leave performance to the backend, missing cross-workflow redundancy that is rampant in batch analytics scenarios where the same SOP-style DAG runs against hundreds of parameter instantiations. Halo represents each workflow as a structured query-plan DAG with explicit LLM and CPU tool nodes, builds a consolidated graph for the whole batch, and optimizes placement/ordering/batching via an epoch-based dynamic-programming solver. The cost model decomposes latency into CPU preparation (T_prep), GPU model-switch (T_model), and inference with prefix-cache discount (T_infer), so the solver simultaneously avoids GPU starvation, model thrashing, and KV-cache fragmentation. The runtime Processor preserves plan intent via an event-driven Coordinator while absorbing stochasticity through wavefront execution, bounded-concurrency CPU scheduling, and constrained work-stealing. Across 6 workloads on IMDb/FineWiki/TPC-H with Qwen3-14B/32B and GPT-OSS-20B on 3 H200 GPUs, Halo delivers up to 3.6x batch speedup and 2.6x online QPS over SOTA baselines (vLLM, OpWise, LangGraph, AgentScope, Parrot), matches MILP-oracle plan quality at 2322x lower solver time (W1: 2.24s vs 1.44h), and cuts cumulative GPU-seconds by ~2x versus OpWise. Ablation shows Request Coalescing is the dominant contributor on complex fan-out topologies (+154% W6), followed by Opportunistic Execution (+56%).
key_findings: Structural redundancy in batch agentic workloads (identical SQL templates, shared prompt prefixes) is the largest optimization headroom: Request Coalescing alone saves 154% on W6 TPCH-Fanout.
Epoch-based DP with topological frontier pruning matches MILP plan quality exactly (Opt=1.0) at >2000x lower solver cost, making workflow-aware global scheduling online-tractable.
Hidden GPU costs (model weight reload, cold KV cache) must be explicit in the cost model; greedy HEFT which ignores them degrades to Opt=0.2 and 1.41x slowdown on complex W6.
High instantaneous GPU utilization (OpWise sawtooth 0-100%) is not high efficiency: cumulative GPU-seconds are the correct billing metric, and Halo uses ~2x fewer (14690 vs 28889 unit.s).
Scalability is near-linear in both batch size (256-2048) and GPU count (1-3 H200) on a single node; baselines saturate due to synchronization barriers.
Pure-LLM chains (W+ linear 3-node) see minimal benefit (1.09x), confirming Halo's niche is tool-rich batch analytics rather than general serving.
limitations: Single-machine multi-GPU only; multi-node deployment requires redesigning cost model for network, KV migration, and weight broadcast.
Fixed logical DAG topology; no support for ReAct / planner-driven / conditional / iterative agentic loops which dominate interactive agents.
Cost model relies on moving-average profiling without drift-detection or replanning guarantees when workload distribution shifts.
Objective is makespan + aggregate load only; no per-request SLO support (unlike Murakkab).
Evaluation is on synthetic workflows (IMDb/FineWiki/TPC-H templates); production long-tail diverse requests not validated.
Anonymous source repo at submission time; real-world adoption and license unclear.

infra_impact.algorithm: N/A — no training-algorithm changes.
infra_impact.kernel: N/A — reuses vLLM PagedAttention and FlashAttention kernels unchanged.
infra_impact.framework: Major — demonstrates that serving engines need a workflow-aware query-optimizer shim on top; pressures vLLM/SGLang to expose resident-model and KV-cache metadata APIs for external planners.
infra_impact.llm: N/A — model-agnostic, validated across 0.4B-32B sizes.
infra_impact.agent: Major — redirects agent-orchestration frameworks (LangGraph, AutoGen, AgentScope) from black-box API composition toward compile-to-plan backends with global scheduling.
infra_impact.code: Anonymous repo at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/Halo_Demo-BC86/; open-source status TBD.

open_questions: Can epoch-based DP be extended to dynamic, replannable workflows (ReAct, planner-driven loops) while keeping solver time bounded?
How does the cost model generalize to multi-node settings with network latency and cross-node KV migration?
Can a learned cost model (e.g., GNN over consolidated DAG) replace moving-average profiling for faster workload-drift adaptation?
What is the integration path with logical-rewrite optimizers (Palimpzest, LOTUS, DocETL) that do model-cascading and prompt-rewriting?
Can SLO-aware objectives (a la Murakkab) be fused into Halo's epoch cost function without breaking DP tractability?
What is the worst-case blowup of topological frontier pruning on extreme fan-in / fan-out DAGs?

papers.title: Kimi K2: Open Agentic Intelligence
papers.core_contribution: 开源1T参数MoE模型Kimi K2，提出MuonClip优化器(Muon+QK-clip)解决训练不稳定性，配合大规模agentic数据合成pipeline和joint RL，在non-thinking设定下达到开源SOTA
papers.summary: Kimi K2是一个32B activated/1T total参数的MoE大语言模型。提出MuonClip优化器——在Muon基础上引入QK-clip技术(当attention logit超过阈值时rescale Q/K权重)解决训练不稳定性。K2在15.5T tokens上预训练，零loss spike。后训练包含三阶段agentic数据合成(tool spec生成→agent/task生成→trajectory生成)和joint RL(verifiable rewards gym + self-critique rubric reward)。K2在多个agentic benchmark上达到SOTA(Tau2-Bench 66.1, SWE-Bench Verified 65.8)，同时在coding/math/reasoning上也表现优秀(LiveCodeBench v6 53.7, AIME 2025 49.5)，成为最强开源non-thinking模型。
key_findings: MuonClip中QK-clip是self-deactivating的——12.7% heads在前70k步触发，训练30%后所有logits自然降到τ=100以下
Sparsity 48(384/8专家)比sparsity 8减少1.69x FLOPs达到相同validation loss
64 attention heads vs 128仅损失0.5-1.2% validation loss但节省83% inference FLOPs at 128k
Agentic数据合成pipeline从3000+真实MCP工具扩展到20000+合成工具
Self-critique rubric reward使RL超越纯验证信号，closed-loop优化critic
LMSYS Arena排名开源第一、总榜第五(3000+投票)
limitations: 在hard reasoning任务上可能生成过多token
不必要启用tool use时性能下降
one-shot全项目生成弱于agentic框架使用
SimpleQA(31.0)显著落后GPT-4.1(42.3)——事实性知识仍有差距

papers.title: KVFlow: Efficient Prefix Caching for Accelerating LLM-Based Multi-Agent Workflows
papers.core_contribution: Workflow-aware KV cache management for multi-agent LLM serving: replaces LRU with steps-to-execution priorities over the radix tree (min aggregation on shared prefixes), adds proactive CPU-GPU prefetching and 4-state status-aware scheduling to fully overlap PCIe transfer with GPU compute.
papers.summary: KVFlow observes that the standard LRU eviction policy used by SGLang/vLLM misbehaves in cyclic or sequential agentic workflows, because the 'most recently used' agent is exactly the one about to execute next. It introduces the Agent Step Graph abstraction where each agent node is assigned a step aggregation function (max+1 for AND-join, min+1 for OR-branch) that unifies DAG, cycle, and conditional branch topologies and computes each agent's steps-to-execution (STE). These STE values are propagated down to individual KV nodes in the radix tree using min aggregation for shared prefixes, enabling fine-grained eviction that preserves soon-to-be-reused entries. On top of that, KVFlow adds proactive CPU-to-GPU prefetching that exploits PCIe full-duplex to overlap transfer with GPU compute, and a 4-state status machine (in-GPU, in-CPU, loading, offloading) with a status-aware scheduler that bypasses requests whose KV is still loading so the GPU never stalls. Implemented as a patch on SGLang v0.4.4, KVFlow achieves up to 1.83x speedup over SGLang+HiCache on single workflows with large (8192-token) prompts, and up to 2.19x over HiCache under 64 concurrent workflows (where HiCache itself regresses to 0.57x of plain SGLang due to reactive-load thrashing). The speedup scales with fixed-prompt length (1.28x at 4096 fixed, 1.48x at 8192 fixed) and diminishes when decode dominates runtime.
key_findings: LRU is structurally wrong for cyclic/sequential agentic workflows: recency anti-correlates with next-use in workflow topologies.
Agent Step Graph with per-node aggregation functions (max+1 / min+1) uniformly handles DAG, cycle, and branch without separate graph-structure treatment.
KV-node-level priority with min aggregation resolves shared-prefix conflicts that agent-level priorities cannot handle.
PCIe full-duplex enables truly zero-contention overlap of CPU->GPU prefetch with GPU->CPU offload/sampling, previously untapped in prefix-cache systems.
Proactive prefetching alone is insufficient under high concurrency (prefetch slower than compute); a 4-state status machine + scheduler bypass is required.
Speedup scales linearly with fixed-prompt length (miss cost is O(n^2) prefill, load is O(n) PCIe), and shrinks as output length grows (decode dominates).
HiCache (SGLang's reactive CPU-cache extension) can regress below plain SGLang at high concurrency because reactive load-back disrupts the layer-by-layer pipeline.
limitations: Assumes workflow graph is static and observable at request time; does not handle ReAct/Reflexion-style dynamic agent programs whose next agent is decided at runtime.
No CPU-side capacity management or secondary eviction; CPU memory can itself exhaust under very high concurrency.
Evaluation is single-GPU only; distributed/multi-node prefix-cache migration is not addressed.
Does not resolve the underlying SGLang KV fragmented layout; only better overlaps the sub-peak PCIe bandwidth.
Reports only average E2E latency; no TTFT/TPOT/P99 or tail-latency distribution.
Tight coupling to radix-tree cache, SGLang frontend (sgl.function), and the presence of CPU second-tier cache.
No ablation separating eviction-only vs prefetch-only vs scheduling contributions.

infra_impact.algorithm: Implies agentic-workflow autotuners (AFlow, Cognify) should co-design cache-friendly topologies with serving-layer priorities.
infra_impact.kernel: N/A - pure cache/scheduler layer, no new kernels required; reuses SGLang attention.
infra_impact.framework: Template for passing application-layer graph metadata into serving backends; natural candidate for vLLM v1 pluggable prefix-cache eviction policies.
infra_impact.llm: Model-architecture agnostic (MHA/GQA/MLA all benefit); long-prompt agents benefit most due to larger KV footprint and higher miss cost.
infra_impact.agent: Plug-in compatible with static-DAG frameworks (MetaGPT, AutoGen graph, LangGraph); requires on-the-fly graph extension for dynamic agents (ReAct, Reflexion).
infra_impact.cluster: Single-node only today; cross-node STE propagation and workflow-aware routing are open follow-up directions.
infra_impact.code: Implemented as an SGLang v0.4.4 patch touching radix-tree eviction, request scheduler, and frontend HTTP metadata; not yet merged upstream.

open_questions: How to extend Agent Step Graph to dynamic agent programs (ReAct/Reflexion) with runtime-decided next steps?
How to manage a secondary eviction policy when CPU memory itself is exhausted (three-tier cache with NVMe)?
How to migrate prefix caches across nodes in multi-GPU/multi-node deployments and propagate STE over RDMA?
What is the joint speedup when combined with speculative decoding or KV sparsity in decode-heavy agentic workloads?
Can workflow optimizers (AFlow, Cognify) incorporate a KVFlow-predicted miss-cost term to generate cache-friendly topologies?
Would reporting TTFT/TPOT/P99 reveal starvation of long-load requests under status-aware bypass scheduling?

papers.title: Mitigating Posterior Salience Attenuation in Long-Context LLMs with Positional Contrastive Decoding
papers.core_contribution: 提出 Positional Contrastive Decoding (PCD)：通过对 RoPE 低频分量做过旋转得到 local-aware logits 并与标准 logits 对比，training-free 地缓解长上下文中 gold token 的 Posterior Salience Attenuation 现象。
papers.summary: 论文识别出长上下文 LLM 解码空间中的 Posterior Salience Attenuation (PSA) 现象——随 context 长度增加，gold token 后验显著度单调衰减但其 rank 仍保持在 top-8 内。基于此观察，作者设计训练免费的 Positional Contrastive Decoding (PCD)：用标准 RoPE base B 计算 long-aware logits L，再用降低后的 base B' (B/B'=1e4) 通过过渡函数 T(x)=2-exp(αx) 从高频到低频逐步注入 over-rotation，得到 local-aware logits L*；最后在 top-γ 候选上做 L̃=(1+β)L-βL* 形成对比解码。理论上证明 contrastive score 的衰减率被压缩到 (lnB'/lnB)^(2/d)<1。在 Llama-3-8B-262K 上实验：InfiniteBench KV Retrieval 8K 段从 72.0→79.0 (+7.0%)、16K 段 +3.0%；RULER VT 8K +6.71；LongBench 平均 +0.89，全面优于 DoLa/MsPoE/Beam-Search/SegR/Rephrasing 等 training-free baseline。方法对任何 RoPE-based LLM 即插即用，主要代价是双 forward 推理。
key_findings: 识别出 PSA 现象：长上下文下 gold token 显著度衰减但 rank 仍处 top-8 — 是排序失误而非召回失败
提出 PCD：用 RoPE base 对比构造 local-aware logits 并从标准 logits 中减去，等价于放大 long-range 信号
理论证明 contrastive 衰减率被改善为 (lnB'/lnB)^(2/d)<1（Theorem A.5）
Llama-3-8B-262K 上 InfiniteBench KV Retrieval 8K 提升 +7.0%、16K 提升 +3.0%；超越 DoLa/MsPoE/Beam-Search 等所有 training-free baseline
敏感超参仅 β（contrast 强度，最佳 2.5），其余 α、γ、B/B' 均稳定，部署调参成本低
高频 RoPE 不能扰动（导致 model collapse），只能扰动低频 — 利用了 RoPE 的频率分工特性
limitations: 不能扩展模型 attention window — 治标不治本，对超出 train context 的输入无效
短文本几乎无收益，甚至 -1.4% 退步；method 在 model-native context 内启用风险高
强绑定 RoPE — 对 ALiBi/KERPLE/NoPE/Mamba+Attn 混合架构不适用
双 forward 推理使 throughput 接近减半；除非定制 kernel 共享 K projection
缺失与 LongRoPE/YaRN 等长上下文 RoPE scaling 方法的横向对比
代码未公开，工程接入细节（KV cache 共享、batch 处理）需读者自行推断

infra_impact.algorithm: 新增 'positional contrastive decoding' 这一类与 DoLa（layer-contrast）、CD（model-contrast）并列的对比解码方法，启发未来 cross-method contrast 研究
infra_impact.kernel: 可受益于 'dual RoPE base 融合 attention kernel'；当前实现需两次完整 attention forward，未来 FlashAttention 扩展可优化
infra_impact.framework: vLLM/SGLang sampler 需扩展 contrastive logits processor；KV cache 需存两份 rotated K（或重算 RoPE）；可参考 DoLa 在 transformers 的 LogitsProcessor 实现模板
infra_impact.llm: 任意 RoPE-based LLM (Llama/Qwen/Mistral/DeepSeek/GLM) 即插即用，无需重训练；MoE 模型也兼容（只改 attention 层）
infra_impact.agent: 长上下文 agent (multi-turn tool use, document QA, code RAG) 直接受益，特别是 8K-32K 中段衰减明显的场景
infra_impact.code: N/A — 论文未公开实现，可在 transformers LogitsProcessor API 上以约 100-200 行代码实现

open_questions: 在 1M+ context 模型上 PCD 是否仍有效？论文最长测到 16K-26K
PCD 与 long-context training (LongRoPE/YaRN/ProLong) 收益是否可叠加？
对非 RoPE 位置编码 (ALiBi/KERPLE/NoPE) 的频率分工假设如何泛化？
amateur model + PCD 双重对比是否能进一步提升 long-range 信号？
实际部署中 KV cache 是否能共享 K projection 让推理开销 < 2x？

papers.title: SageAttention2++: A More Efficient Implementation of SageAttention2
papers.core_contribution: Uses FP16-accumulated FP8 MMA instructions with narrowed quantization ranges to achieve 3.9x speedup over FlashAttention2 with no accuracy loss.
papers.summary: SageAttention2++ switches from FP32-accumulated FP8 instructions (2x over FP16) to FP16-accumulated FP8 instructions (4x over FP16) for PV matmul. Narrowing FP8 quantization ranges for P (224) and V (4.5) keeps accumulated products within FP16 range. Delayed FP32 Buffering halves type conversion overhead. Achieves 3.9x speedup over FlashAttention2 on RTX4090 at 32K sequence length with CosSim 99.97%.
key_findings: FP8 with FP16 accumulator is 4x faster than FP16 (vs 2x for FP32 accumulator)
Narrowing FP8 quantization ranges maintains identical accuracy (CosSim 99.97%)
Delayed FP32 Buffering halves FP16→FP32 conversion overhead
3.9x speedup over FlashAttention2 on RTX4090
limitations: Only evaluated on NVIDIA Ada/Blackwell consumer GPUs
FP16 accumulator instruction not available on Hopper
Only optimizes PV Matmul; QK unchanged
Very short paper with limited ablation

infra_impact.algorithm: Enables faster attention without algorithmic changes
infra_impact.kernel: Demonstrates practical use of FP16-accumulated FP8 MMA; generalizable to bounded-output FP8 GEMM
infra_impact.framework: Drop-in replacement for FlashAttention2 in inference pipelines
infra_impact.llm: Directly accelerates prefill phase for long-context applications
infra_impact.agent: Reduced latency enables faster real-time inference
infra_impact.cluster: N/A

open_questions: Can FP16 accumulator tricks be applied to datacenter GPUs?
What is the theoretical limit of quantization range narrowing?
Can this be combined with sparse attention for multiplicative speedups?

papers.title: SageAttention3: Microscaling FP4 Attention for Inference and An Exploration of 8-Bit Training
papers.core_contribution: First FP4 microscaling attention kernel achieving 1038 TOPS on RTX5090 (5x FlashAttention) via two-level quantization, plus the first trainable 8-bit attention (SageBwd) enabling lossless fine-tuning.
papers.summary: SageAttention3 leverages Blackwell's FP4 Tensor Cores with NVFP4 microscaling quantization. Two-level quantization for softmax output P expands [0,1] range to use more E4M3 scale values, improving CosSim from 93.32% to 99.52%. SageBwd extends INT8 per-block quantization to attention backward, keeping the most sensitive GEMM (dO·V^T) in FP16. Results: 1038 TOPS on RTX5090, 3x end-to-end HunyuanVideo speedup, lossless fine-tuning but slower pretraining convergence.
key_findings: FP4 attention achieves 1038 TOPS on RTX5090, 5x faster than FlashAttention2
Two-level quantization improves softmax output FP4 CosSim from 93.32% to 99.52%
NVFP4 significantly outperforms MXFP4 for attention quantization
INT8 trainable attention (SageBwd) matches BF16 in fine-tuning but converges slower in pretraining
dO·V^T is the most accuracy-sensitive backward GEMM — must remain FP16
HunyuanVideo 3x speedup (489s→164s) with no quality degradation
limitations: FP4 only works on Blackwell GPUs, no backward compatibility
SageBwd pretraining convergence is notably slower — not viable for pretraining
SageBwd actual speedup (1.1-1.15x) far below theoretical due to Triton implementation
No evaluation on LLM long-context prefill scenarios

infra_impact.algorithm: Proves low-bit attention viable for fine-tuning; pretraining convergence issue opens new research direction
infra_impact.kernel: First FP4 attention kernel; two-level quantization is generalizable FP4 softmax technique
infra_impact.framework: Plug-and-play PyTorch API replacing FlashAttention; CUTLASS+CUDA inference, Triton training
infra_impact.llm: Massive speedup for video/image generation; fine-tuning acceleration; LLM prefill impact to be validated
infra_impact.agent: 5x attention speedup significantly reduces real-time generation latency
infra_impact.cluster: N/A

open_questions: Can mixed-precision scheduling solve pretraining convergence?
How does FP4 attention perform in LLM prefill and decode?
Can two-level quantization extend to FP4 GEMM beyond attention?
Can SageBwd be ported to AMD CDNA4?

papers.title: HEXGEN-FLOW: Optimizing LLM Inference Request Scheduling for Agentic Text-to-SQL
papers.core_contribution: Two-level scheduler (global workload-balanced dispatch + local urgency-based priority queue with propagating SLO budgets) that upgrades LLM serving scheduling from per-request to per-workflow granularity for agentic multi-stage workloads on heterogeneous GPU clusters.
papers.summary: HexGen-Flow targets agentic Text-to-SQL workloads (CHESS/MAC-SQL) where each user query triggers ~20 dependent LLM calls across 4 stages on heterogeneous GPU pools (A100+A6000+L40). Existing schedulers (vLLM FCFS+RR, VTC, QLM) treat LLM requests as independent tasks, ignoring stage dependencies, hardware heterogeneity, and end-to-end SLO propagation. HexGen-Flow introduces a hierarchical scheduler: a global coordinator dispatches each request via a non-linear score Score=(1-α)·β/t_queue - α·t_comp balancing task suitability against load; each instance runs a local priority queue ordered by urgency U=t_comp-(t_SLO-τ_queue), where per-stage SLO budgets are allocated proportionally to average compute cost and recomputed whenever an upstream stage completes (so upstream overruns automatically tighten downstream budgets). α is auto-tuned every 100s via a lightweight CPU simulator triggered by t-test-detected P95 regression. On BIRD-bench with Llama3.1-70B across three deployments, HexGen-Flow reduces P95 tail latency by 1.42-1.56x and improves throughput by 1.49-1.81x over vLLM/VTC/QLM; ablations confirm WB contributes 10.5-34.8% and PQ up to 48% P95 reduction; MAC-SQL+Qwen3-30B on Spider shows 34-38% P95 improvement, confirming generalization beyond Text-to-SQL.
key_findings: Workflow-aware SLO budget propagation (budgets reallocated on every stage completion) is the core primitive that makes per-request urgency metrics robust to estimation errors.

On heterogeneous GPUs, round-robin dispatching is actively harmful: case study shows RR sends 43.9% of stage-1 (light) requests to A100 while WB correctly routes 57.5% of stage-2 (heavy) requests to A100 instead.
Optimal α drifts with hardware and workload (Hetero-1: 0.1-0.2; Hetero-2: 0.3-0.4), mandating online auto-tuning; CPU simulator overhead is only 115-158s, negligible versus hour-scale workload drift.
WB and PQ are orthogonal and both necessary: neither alone matches the full WB+PQ system; WB alone reduces P95 by 10.5-34.8%, PQ alone by up to 48%.

Priority queue makes counter-intuitive decisions (selects request arriving at t=64.4s with urgency 26.9 over request arriving at t=22.4s with urgency 14.5) — this FCFS violation is what drives SLO attainment gains.
The scheduling framework generalizes beyond Text-to-SQL: swapping to MAC-SQL + Qwen3-30B on Spider traces still yields 34-38% P95 improvement.
limitations: Relies on accurate output length prediction (Zheng et al. 2023); no analysis of what prediction MAPE causes scheduling to collapse.
Assumes static workflow DAG — dynamic agentic workflows (e.g., LangGraph runtime stage expansion) break the proportional SLO budget allocation.
Preemption overhead (KV cache swap/evict in vLLM) is not separately profiled, so PQ's reported 48% P95 gain may be gross rather than net.
No comparison with modern multi-instance schedulers (Llumnix, DistServe, Mooncake) — only compared against weaker baselines (vLLM FCFS+RR, VTC, QLM).
α-tuning assumes workload stationarity over 100s windows; bursty traffic (flash sales, retry storms) can invalidate simulator trace.
Single centralized coordinator is a scaling bottleneck; no discussion of sharding for 100+ instance deployments.
Doesn't address prefill-decode disaggregation or prefix caching, both now standard in modern serving stacks.

infra_impact.algorithm: Enables productionization of inference-time scaling algorithms (best-of-N, self-correction, iterative refinement) by giving them SLO guarantees at the serving layer.
infra_impact.kernel: N/A — built atop vLLM, does not modify CUDA kernels or attention implementations.
infra_impact.framework: Direct blueprint for next-generation serving frameworks: vLLM/SGLang should expose workflow-DAG APIs and SLO budget propagation primitives so upper-layer agent frameworks can inject stage dependencies.
infra_impact.llm: N/A for model design; indirectly supports long-CoT and multi-step reasoning models by making their multi-call deployment tractable.
infra_impact.agent: Milestone for agentic LLM serving: first systematic workflow-aware scheduler. Reference implementation for AutoGen / LangGraph / CrewAI production deployments.
infra_impact.cluster: Targets enterprise heterogeneous GPU pools explicitly (A100 + A6000 + L40); complements cluster-level scheduling research like Llumnix by handling workflow-level orchestration.
infra_impact.code: Open source at github.com/Relaxed-System-Lab/Hexgen-Flow; usable as a reference agentic serving scheduler built on vLLM.

open_questions: How does HexGen-Flow compose with Llumnix's dynamic instance migration and DistServe's PD disaggregation — orthogonal gain or conflict?
At what output-length-prediction MAPE does the SLO budget allocation collapse, and can uncertainty-aware predictors (conformal prediction, Bayesian) improve robustness?
What is the net benefit of PQ preemption after accounting for KV cache eviction/swap overhead in vLLM?
How does the centralized coordinator scale to 100+ instances, and what sharding strategy preserves cross-instance workload visibility?
Can the workflow-aware scheduling primitive be extended to RLHF/GRPO training (rollout → reward → update as multi-stage workflow)?
How does the system handle dynamic agentic DAGs where runtime decisions add new stages (tool calls, branching reasoning)?

papers.title: DynaServe: Unified and Elastic Execution for Dynamic Disaggregated LLM Serving
papers.core_contribution: A micro-request abstraction that splits LLM requests at arbitrary token boundaries with a two-level scheduling framework, unifying colocation and disaggregation paradigms for maximizing goodput under strict SLO constraints.
papers.summary: LLM serving faces tension between low tail latency and high throughput. DynaServe introduces the micro-request abstraction allowing requests to be split at any token boundary into cooperating segments. A global scheduler uses bounded binary search for optimal split points, while local schedulers compose SLO-aware batches. Chunk-based KV cache transfers overlap communication with computation. On A100 clusters, DynaServe achieves 1.15x-3.07x higher serving capacity and up to 1.91x/1.61x higher goodput vs colocation/disaggregation.
key_findings: Neither colocation nor disaggregation handles dynamic unbalanced workloads well
Micro-request abstraction generalizes both paradigms as special cases
Two-level scheduling achieves near-optimal performance with <20ms per-request overhead
SLO-aware local batching raises SLO attainment from 52% to 99%
Chunk-based KV transfer reduces non-overlapped transfer time by 94%
DynaServe is robust to output length prediction errors (only 2.9% goodput drop at σ=100)
limitations: Requires output length prediction which introduces error
Evaluated only on A100 GPUs
Built atop vLLM — porting to other engines may require significant effort
Limited to two-way splits — multi-way partitioning unexplored

papers.title: JITServe: SLO-aware LLM Serving with Imprecise Request Information
papers.core_contribution: First SLO-aware LLM serving system that maximizes service goodput across latency/deadline/compound workloads by using conservative quantile upper-bound length predictions (QRF) plus pattern-graph dependency matching, scheduled by a Grouped Margin Goodput Maximization (GMAX) algorithm with a provable ~1/8.55 competitive ratio against the optimal oracle.
papers.summary: JITServe targets the growing diversity of LLM workloads (streaming chat, deadline-sensitive agent tool calls, and compound multi-stage requests such as deep research and multi-agent systems) where existing schedulers optimizing aggregate throughput (vLLM FCFS, Sarathi-Serve) or mean E2EL (Autellix LAS, LTR SJF) fail to satisfy per-request SLOs — the paper proves SJF and EDF have arbitrarily bad competitive ratios against an oracle and that the underlying optimal scheduling is NP-hard (Multiple Knapsack reduction). Its approach accepts that precise response-length and dependency prediction is infeasible (BERT/Llama3 predictors systematically under-predict) and instead uses a Quantile Regression Forest to produce a high-quantile upper bound on length in 7ms, re-invoked every 50 tokens for online refinement, plus a pattern-graph matcher (K-medoids + Gaussian kernel) that infers sub-deadlines for compound requests by a cumulative-share formulation phi(s) = t_<=s / t_total. The GMAX scheduler then prioritizes each request by margin goodput per unit bandwidth (Priority = goodput(r)/t_gen(r), invariant to frame size), applies top-p filtering to protect goodput floor, and uses an input-length sliding window of size B to jointly maximize goodput and batch homogeneity (critical because heterogeneous-length batches remain slow even with Flash Decoding). The system is built as a vLLM middleware with only a few API changes (deadline, target_tbt, target_ttft, waiting_time), offloads Request Analyzer to an async gRPC process, and proves a constant competitive ratio ~1/8.55 under delta=10% preemption threshold via credit-charging + preemption-chain amortization. On 16×A100 with Llama-3.1-8B/70B, Qwen2.5-14B, Qwen3-30B-MoE-A3B across chat/deep-research/agentic-codegen/math workloads and 10K+ requests per run, JITServe improves service goodput 1.4x-6.3x over vLLM/Sarathi-Serve/Autellix/LTR, saves 28.5%-83.2% resources for equivalent goodput, keeps throughput at 96-98% of Sarathi-Serve, and sits within 3-9% of an oracle with perfect foresight.
key_findings: SJF and EDF scheduling have arbitrarily bad competitive ratios against the oracle; Appendix D.1 constructs adversarial workloads where OPT/SJF and OPT/EDF are unbounded.
Upper-bound length prediction (QRF P95) is 7x faster than BERT (7ms vs 49ms) and unlike point predictors never under-predicts, which is what makes SLO-safe scheduling possible.
Heterogeneous-length batches remain 20-40% slower per-token even with Flash Decoding, forcing GMAX to group by input length as a separate scheduling dimension orthogonal to goodput priority.
JITServe's two-step GMAX decoupling (top-p priority filter + input-length sliding window) admits a provable ~1/8.55 competitive ratio via credit-charging + preemption-chain amortization.
Experimentally within 3-9% of an oracle with perfect length+dependency knowledge, effectively ceiling-ing the value of better length predictors for goodput-oriented serving.
Beats Sarathi-Serve by 1.72x even on its home-turf latency-only workload, and matches Sarathi on raw throughput (96-98%), meaning scheduling sophistication does not cost throughput here.
limitations: QRF requires historical prompt-to-response-length labeled data; cold-start accuracy on brand-new models or never-seen agent workflows is not quantified.
Pattern-graph matching degrades to uniform sub-deadline allocation on truly novel workflow structures with no similar historical graphs.
Evaluated only on 16xA100 + vLLM; no measurement on H100, MI300X/MI355X, SGLang, or TRT-LLM backends where TMA and dynamic shapes may weaken the batch-length-homogeneity assumption.
External SLOs are a mandatory input; the system does not infer deadlines from application context.
No discussion of multi-tenant fairness, billing model, or how conservative bandwidth reservation interacts with pricing tiers.
No integration with prefill-decode disaggregation (DistServe, MoonCake) where the frame-based bandwidth model needs to be rewritten across pools.

infra_impact.algorithm: Establishes goodput as a first-class scheduling objective and provides a pattern-graph + sub-deadline template that can inform online RL and agentic RL training systems.
infra_impact.kernel: Does not require new kernels, but the finding that batch-length heterogeneity hurts even with Flash Decoding motivates future length-heterogeneous attention kernels.
infra_impact.framework: Direct push to make SLO (deadline/target_tbt/target_ttft/waiting_time) a first-class API in vLLM/SGLang-style stacks, aligned with OpenAI-style request parameters.
infra_impact.llm: Model-architecture agnostic (dense + MoE validated); but QRF accuracy depends on response-length distribution stability, which may degrade for reasoning models (o1/R1).
infra_impact.agent: Largest incremental value: pattern-graph matching + cumulative-share sub-deadline allocation provides a native E2E deadline management primitive for multi-agent/deep-research workflows, previously absent.
infra_impact.code: Minimal integration footprint (vLLM scheduler policy module + async gRPC analyzer); however the QRF training pipeline is not open-sourced, the primary reproduction barrier.

open_questions: How does QRF behave on reasoning models (o1/R1/DeepSeek-R1) where response length has heavy-tail variance and weak correlation with prompt features?
Can GMAX be extended to prefill-decode disaggregated serving (DistServe/MoonCake) where the frame-based bandwidth model needs cross-pool decomposition?
How should multi-tenant fairness and billing be folded into the Priority = goodput/t_gen formulation without breaking the competitive-ratio proof?
What is the QRF cold-start behavior for brand-new models, and can LLM-as-predictor or retrieval-augmented QRF provide a zero-shot fallback?
Does the batch-length-homogeneity benefit persist on H100/MI350 where TMA + dynamic shape reduce the penalty of mixed-length batches?
How does JITServe interact with KV-cache migration and mid-request failover (Llumnix/SpotServe-style)?

papers.title: Qwen2.5-Omni Technical Report
papers.core_contribution: 首个开源的 omni-modal 端到端模型 (text+image+audio+video → text+speech)。Thinker-Talker 架构: Thinker (dense 7B-class, 28L × 3584, GQA 28/4, FFN 18944) 处理理解, Talker (dense 24L × 896, dual-track AR, vocab 8448) 直接消费 Thinker hidden + token embed 驱动 codec; TM-RoPE (M-RoPE [16,24,24] + 40ms 绝对时间) 对齐音视频; Flow-Matching DiT (22L × 1024, sliding 4-block) + Modified BigVGAN 实现流式波形合成 @ 24kHz.
papers.summary: Qwen2.5-Omni 是 Qwen3-Omni 的前代, 全 dense + 单 codebook + DiT vocoder。Thinker (init Qwen2.5): 28L × 3584, GQA 28/4 head_dim=128, FFN 18944, vocab 152064, M-RoPE [16, 24, 24], context 32768. Talker (dual-track AR): 24L × 896, GQA 12/4, FFN 18944 (居然和 Thinker 同), 输入 = Thinker layer hidden 3584 + 采样 token embed, 输出单 codec stream vocab 8448. Audio Encoder = Whisper-large-v3 init: 32L × 1280, 20 heads × 64, GELU FFN 5120, 2-sec block attn, mel 128/25ms/10ms. Vision Encoder = Qwen2.5-VL ViT: 32L × 1280, 16 heads × 80, patch 14, fullatt @ [7,15,23,31], spatial_merge=2. Speech chain: qwen-tts-tokenizer (单 codebook 8448) → Flow-Matching DiT (22L × 1024, sliding-window 4 blocks: 2 lookback + current + 1 lookahead) → Modified BigVGAN (upsample [5,3,2,2,2,2]) → PCM 24 kHz. TM-RoPE: M-RoPE 三轴 + 40ms/temporal step + 25 Hz alignment grid + 2-sec chunks (视觉先音频后 interleave). 训练: S1 encoder alignment (LLM frozen, adapter-first then encoder) → S2 joint pretrain (800B image + 300B audio + 100B video-with-audio incremental) → S3 long context 32K. 后训练: ChatML SFT + Talker 4 阶段 (continuation → DPO LDPO with WER+pause → multi-speaker FT → timbre disentangle).
key_findings: Thinker dense 7B: 28×3584, GQA 28/4, head_dim=128, FFN 18944
Talker dense 0.6B: 24×896, GQA 12/4, head_dim=128, FFN 18944, vocab 8448
Talker 输入: Thinker hidden (3584) + 采样 text token embed (紧耦合, Qwen3-Omni 后来解耦)
Audio = Whisper-large-v3 init: 32×1280, 20 heads × head_dim=64, GELU FFN 5120, 2-sec blocks
Vision = Qwen2.5-VL: 32×1280, 16 heads × head_dim=80, fullatt @ [7,15,23,31], spatial_merge=2
TM-RoPE: M-RoPE [16, 24, 24] + 40ms/temporal step, position_id_per_seconds=25
Speech codec: 单 codebook 8448 (qwen-tts-tokenizer)
Vocoder: DiT 22L × 1024 + BigVGAN, sliding 4-block window (2 back + current + 1 fwd)
Joint pretrain (S2): 800B image + 300B audio + 100B video-with-audio (incremental)
Long context: 8192 → 32768 (no YaRN)
Talker post-train: continuation → LDPO (WER + pause reward) → multi-speaker FT → timbre disent
Token rate ~25 Hz (vs Qwen3-Omni 12.5 Hz)
首包延迟 ~640ms+ (DiT 4-block 等 lookahead, vs Qwen3-Omni 234ms)
Apache 2.0 开源
limitations: 模型规模仅 7B，相对闭源模型参数量有限
语音生成基于 sliding-window DiT 计算开销仍较大
音视频 interleaved 排列依赖 2 秒 chunk，限制时间分辨率
语音理解语种支持有限

papers.title: AMD CDNA 4 Architecture White Paper
papers.core_contribution: 引入OCP MX微缩放格式(MXFP8/6/4)硬件支持，Matrix Core翻倍16-bit以下吞吐，LDS扩容至160KB，FP8达5 PFLOPS，MXFP4达10 PFLOPS
papers.summary: CDNA 4在chiplet基础上精细优化：XCD升级至3nm(N3P)，IOD简化为2个(N6)降低延迟和功耗。最大创新是全面支持OCP MX微缩放格式——MXFP8/MXFP6/MXFP4，每32个元素共享一个8-bit缩放因子。Matrix Core对16-bit及以下数据类型吞吐翻倍(FP8: 5.0 PF, MXFP4: 10 PF)。LDS从64KB扩容至160KB并读带宽翻倍。大胆取舍：移除TF32硬件(转软件模拟)，FP64 Matrix性能减半(78.6 TF)。288GB HBM3E, 8TB/s带宽。MI350X(1000W风冷)和MI355X(1400W液冷)两个SKU。

papers.title: Massive Values in Self-Attention Modules are the Key to Contextual Knowledge Understanding
papers.core_contribution: 首次系统性揭示 LLM 中 Q/K 矩阵的 concentrated massive values 由 RoPE 低频维度引起，且这些大值是上下文知识理解（而非参数知识检索）的关键功能性信号，直接指导量化策略选择。
papers.summary: 本文发现使用 RoPE 的 LLM 中，attention Q 和 K 在特定维度区域出现集中的异常大值（V 中不存在），通过 disruption 实验证明这些 massive values 对上下文知识理解至关重要（GSM8K 从 76.9% 暴跌至 4.0%）但对参数知识检索影响有限（仅降 15-20%）。量化实验进一步表明保护 massive values 的方法（AWQ/SmoothQuant）优于不保护的（GPTQ）。因果分析建立了 RoPE 频率衰减 → 低频维度语义积累 → massive values 的完整因果链。
key_findings: Q/K 中 massive values 集中在 RoPE 低频维度，跨注意力头位置一致，V 中无此模式
Massive values 对上下文知识理解至关重要：破坏后 GSM8K 76.9%→4.0%，Passkey 100%→0%
参数知识检索仅受温和影响（-15-20%），说明知识存储与上下文处理存在分离机制
AWQ/SmoothQuant（保护 massive values）在上下文任务上显著优于 GPTQ（不保护）
RoPE 是 concentrated massive values 的根因：无 RoPE 模型不出现此现象
知识冲突实验：破坏 massive values 后模型忽略误导性上下文，回退参数知识
limitations: 仅在 7B-9B 规模模型上实验，未验证 70B+ 或 MoE 模型的一致性
Disruption 方法较粗暴，无法精确量化信息承载量
未探讨 GQA/MQA 等 Q/K 共享机制与 massive values 的交互
知识冲突实验仅在简单事实问答上验证，复杂推理场景未测试

infra_impact.algorithm: 启发基于 massive value 保护的量化感知训练和低频维度正则化方法设计
infra_impact.kernel: 量化 kernel 需对 Q/K 低频维度采用更高精度或 per-channel scaling
infra_impact.framework: 推理框架 KV cache 量化应保护 K 的低频维度；AWQ/SmoothQuant 应优先于 GPTQ
infra_impact.llm: 直接影响架构设计：RoPE 低频维度承载语义信息，可考虑增加容量或精度
infra_impact.agent: Agent 高度依赖上下文理解，使用 massive-value-aware 量化对 agent 性能至关重要
infra_impact.cluster: N/A

非 RoPE 位置编码（ALiBi, NoPE）是否有等价的功能性信号？
能否在训练中主动塑造 massive value 分布以提升特定能力？
RoPE base 参数如何影响 massive value 的分布和功能？

papers.title: NoLiMa: Long-Context Evaluation Beyond Literal Matching
papers.core_contribution: A long-context benchmark that strips literal/lexical overlap between question and needle (ROUGE-L precision ~0.07 vs ~0.86 in vanilla NIAH), revealing that all 13 frontier LLMs claiming 128K-1M context actually have effective length <= 8K (most <= 2K) when forced to rely on latent associative reasoning instead of surface matching.
papers.summary:
NoLiMa extends needle-in-a-haystack with a hand-curated needle set whose questions and needles share zero surface tokens; the link from question keyword Wq to needle keyword Wn must be inferred via world knowledge or commonsense (e.g., "Semper Opera House" -> "Dresden"). Haystacks are 60K+-token shuffles of 10 open-license books, scrubbed by a two-stage pipeline (Contriever distractor filter + iterative Llama-3.3-70B alternative-answer filter, validated at 99.8% control accuracy). Each test ships in 1-hop and 2-hop variants and in default vs inverted word order, totaling 58 question-needle pairs evaluated at 8 context lengths from 250 to 32K tokens (7,540 tests per length).

On 13 frontier LLMs (GPT-4o, Gemini 1.5 Pro / 2.0 Flash, Llama 3.1/3.3 8B-405B, Claude 3.5 Sonnet, Mistral Large 2, Jamba 1.5 Mini, Command R+) effective length (max L with accuracy >= 85% of base score) is at most 8K and typically 1-2K, despite claimed contexts of 128K-2M. By 32K, 11/13 models lose more than half their base accuracy. GPT-4o is strongest but still drops 99.3 -> 69.7. The paper introduces an aligned last-2K placement protocol that holds RoPE relative distance constant across L, isolating attention-load (rather than position-encoding decay) as the dominant cause of failure. Two-hop and inverted-order needles widen the gap further. CoT prompting and reasoning models (GPT-o1, o3-mini, R1-Distill-70B) help mildly but do not close the gap above 16K.

A single distractor sentence containing Wq (irrelevant to the answer) collapses GPT-4o’s effective length from 8K to 1K, demonstrating that current LLMs preferentially attend to surface keyword matches even when they are content-empty. The benchmark, code, and updated NoLiMa-Hard reasoning-model leaderboard are publicly released at https://github.com/adobe-research/NoLiMa.
key_findings: 13/13 frontier LLMs have effective length <= 8K on NoLiMa, 16-128x shorter than their claimed 128K-2M context windows.
ROUGE-L precision between question and relevant context drops from 0.86 (vanilla NIAH) to 0.07 in NoLiMa, isolating latent-association recall from surface matching.
Replacing the latent-link question with a literal one (Direct setup) restores Llama 3.3 70B to 98.5% even at 32K -- the gap is the price of literal matching.
Two-hop reasoning compounds multiplicatively with context length: Llama 3.3 70B at 32K scores 56.2% (1-hop) vs 25.9% (2-hop); GPT-4o 79.8% vs 57.4%.

Aligned last-2K placement (constant RoPE relative distance across L) still shows uniform performance drop with L, ruling out position-encoding decay as the dominant cause -- the bottleneck is attention SNR.
A single distractor sentence containing Wq (irrelevant to the answer) collapses GPT-4o effective length from 8K to 1K -- the strongest evidence that LLMs prefer surface matches over latent meaning.
CoT prompting and reasoning models (GPT-o1, o3-mini, R1-Distill-70B) help by 8-30% relative but cannot maintain performance beyond 16K on NoLiMa-Hard.
GPT-4.1 (Appendix E v3) achieves the best effective length (16K) but still drops below 65% at 128K context.
limitations: Synthetic single-needle setup; does not directly model real RAG / long-doc QA workloads with multiple, partial, or noisy gold passages.
Closed-source models are treated as black boxes -- no attention-head probing or representation analysis can verify the proposed attention-dilution mechanism for them.
Filter LLM (Llama 3.3 70B) is itself fallible; the 99.8% control test bounds detection of intentionally inserted needles but not false negatives on natural-prose alternative answers.
No mechanistic interpretability -- explicitly out of scope; we see that causal attention struggles with inverted/2-hop in long contexts but not which heads/layers fail.
English-only and Western-knowledge anchored; multilingual or culture-shifted variants untested.
Diagnoses the disease but does not test cures (training-data composition, architecture changes, retrieval-augmented attention).
Distractor template ("There was an article about Wq in the daily newspaper") is artificial; generalisation to natural lexical noise in production RAG corpora is plausible but not directly measured.

infra_impact.algorithm: Calls for long-context training data with paraphrased queries / latent-link synthetic to reduce induction-head dominance; suggests post-training recipes that explicitly break literal-match shortcuts (e.g., NoLiMa-style SFT mix).
infra_impact.kernel: No direct change required -- bottleneck is statistical SNR rather than throughput; weakens quality justification for sparse / sliding-window attention since they cannot raise SNR.
infra_impact.framework: Serving stacks (vLLM, SGLang, TRT-LLM) should expose effective-length metadata alongside max context length. RAG retrievers should up-weight semantic relevance over BM25 when feeding low-overlap chunks. Operational rule: tune chunking and stop-conditions around effective length, not claimed length.
infra_impact.llm: Diagnoses universal failure mode in causal Transformer + softmax attention + RoPE under low-SNR regimes (true for dense Llama, GQA, MoE Jamba, MoE Mistral). Motivates research into retrieval-augmented attention, fact-store / Memory Layers, learned sparsity, and training-data interventions to repair effective length.
infra_impact.agent: For agent runtimes piling system prompt + tool docs + memory + observation history into one context window: tools the agent has not lexically referenced recently are likely invisible. Use external memory retrieval rather than long-context recall for stale items; keep recall-critical content in fresh, lexically-keyed positions.
infra_impact.code: Public eval harness at github.com/adobe-research/NoLiMa with vLLM and OpenAI-style API support; needle sets as JSON, pre-shuffled haystacks. Likely to become a standard regression suite alongside RULER and BABILong; NoLiMa-Hard subset is the emerging reasoning-model long-context benchmark (GPT-o3 currently leads at 58.5% / 32K).

open_questions: Is the failure mode attention-mechanism-specific or general to all sequence models? Test on pure Mamba and encoder-decoder models.
Can long-context training data engineered for low ROUGE between query and gold span close the effective-length gap?
Which specific attention heads / layers fail at long L on NoLiMa? Probe induction heads and function vectors as L grows.
How much of GPT-4o’s advantage is training data vs architecture vs RLHF? Replicate on open models with controlled training.
Does retrieval-augmented attention (RetNet, Memory Layers, REALM-style) close the gap or just emulate external RAG?
Why does one distractor sentence collapse GPT-4o effective length 8x? Can models be trained to be robust to such distractors without losing literal-match accuracy?

papers.core_contribution: Systematic characterization and optimization of GPU DMA copy engines for overlapping computation with communication in distributed ML, achieving up to 15% end-to-end training speedup.
