papers.title: Lost in the Middle, and In-Between: Enhancing Language Models' Ability to Reason Over Long Contexts in Multi-Hop QA
papers.core_contribution: 证明长上下文 LLM 的 lost-in-the-middle 现象在多跳 QA 中存在第二层退化——准确率不仅与证据离上下文边缘的距离有关，还与多个证据片段之间的相对距离有关；并系统评估 CoT、文档摘要、KG-triple 三类缓解方案，发现它们均不能同时解决位置偏置与绝对准确率两个目标。
papers.summary:
Liu et al. (2024) 在 single-hop QA 中观察到 long-context LLM 准确率呈 U 形——gold 文档放在中间最差、放在两端最好。本文把该 perturbation 协议升级到 multi-hop 场景：用 HotpotQA / 2WikiMultihopQA / MuSiQue-Ans 三个数据集，把每条问题的 k 个 gold 文档（k=2/3/4）放进 20 文档输入的不同位置，按 adjacent (相邻位 (1,2),(5,6)…) 与 separated (跨距位 (1,5),(5,10)…) 两类 9 个组合扫描。同时叠加文档表示（原文 / BART 摘要 / LLaMA-2 抽取的 KG triple）和 prompt 模式（standard / few-shot CoT），在 MPT-7B-8k-instruct (ALiBi)、Llama-2-7B-longlora-8k-ft、GPT-3.5-turbo-1106 三个模型上系统测试。

核心发现：(1) 跨所有 (model, dataset) 配置，adjacent 配置稳定优于 separated，证明证据间距本身是独立退化变量——'lost in BETWEEN'；(2) few-shot CoT 在 instruction-tuned 模型 (MPT, GPT) 上增益正常但无法压平 U 形曲线，在非指令微调的 Llama-2-longlora 上反而把 HotpotQA 准确率从 51.9% 打到 0.6%（primacy bias 让模型过度抄写示例）；(3) Summary 与 KG 缩短上下文确实能压平位置曲线，但绝对准确率下跌 15–30 pp，弱模型反倒能从中受益。

论文未提出新算法，是一篇 diagnostic-first 的负面结论 paper，但贡献了一个新的评测维度——multi-hop 场景下的 'between-distance' 退化——这将影响下一代长上下文模型的 robustness benchmark 与 RAG/Agent 框架的 evidence packing 策略。
key_findings: 多跳 QA 中 LLM 位置偏置是两层叠加：绝对位置 U 形（lost-in-middle）+ gold 文档间距单调退化（lost-in-between），后者跨 6 个 (model, dataset) 配置无反例。
在 GPT-3.5 HotpotQA 2-hop 上，相同起点位置 1，第二个 gold 从位置 2 移到位置 5 即掉 2.9 pp 准确率，证明 between-distance 效应即使在 1-2 跳的最简单设定下也能测得。
few-shot CoT 在 long-context 模型上是双刃剑——指令微调模型增益正常，但非指令微调的 Llama-2-longlora 在 CoT 下出现 degenerate generation，准确率塌方至 ≤1%。
Summary 和 KG triple 抽取能压平位置曲线，但绝对准确率下跌 15–30 pp（GPT-3.5 HotpotQA: full 73.9 → KG 54.3 → Summary 59.5），仅对弱模型 net-positive。
现有缓解策略（reranking、long-context fine-tuning、permutation self-consistency）在多跳下因组合空间 C(20, k) 爆炸（k=4 时 4845 种）变得工程不可行。
评测协议贡献：把 single-hop 的 perturbation 框架推广到 multi-hop 的 (位置 × 间距) 二维空间，可被复用为新一代长上下文 LLM 的 robustness 回归测试。
limitations: 未评估 GPT-4o / Llama-3.1-128k / Claude-3 / Gemini-1.5 等 2024 下半年之后的新一代长上下文模型，结论是否对位置编码与训练数据更新后的模型仍成立未知。
仅采样 9 个位置组合，远小于 C(20, k) 的全空间（k=4 时 4845 种），位置曲线只是骨架而非稠密刻画。
未提出新的缓解算法，是 diagnostic-only paper，Future Work 中 dynamic CoT、external memory 都只是 hand-wave。
CoT 只用了 least-to-most 一种 prompt，未覆盖 self-consistency / Tree-of-Thoughts，CoT 失败结论的外推性受限。
best-subspan accuracy 对长输出有偏（输出越长越易包含答案串），CoT 模式下的高分可能部分来自该度量偏置，论文未做 ablation。
summarization 用 BART-large-CNN（新闻摘要模型）对维基类问答未必最优；HotpotQA 也存在训练数据污染嫌疑（GPT-3.5 closed-book 已能拿 40.6%），论文未做对照。

infra_impact.algorithm: 定义了 multi-hop 'lost-in-between' 这一新失败模式，会被后续 long-context 训练算法（NTK scaling、YaRN、position interpolation 下一代变体）作为必测的 robustness 评测项；提示需要在训练时显式做位置组合数据增强或 RL 直接奖励 chain robustness。
infra_impact.kernel: N/A — 论文不涉及任何算子或 kernel 改动。
infra_impact.framework: RAG / serving 框架应在 retrieval 后增加 evidence-packing reranker：按主题/实体相似度将 multi-hop chain 中的 gold 段落聚簇相邻；vLLM/SGLang 的 RAG demo 应增加 pack-adjacent-gold 选项。
infra_impact.llm: 长上下文模型评测应纳入 multi-hop 位置 benchmark；位置编码（ALiBi vs RoPE）和指令微调质量都直接影响曲线形状，应在 model card 公开 between-distance 鲁棒性指标。
infra_impact.agent: RAG agent 的 evidence selection 应优先 'fewer but semantically clustered' 而非 'more but scattered'；本文为 agent 的 evidence 排序策略提供了 quantitative 依据，并警示对非指令微调模型不宜直接套用 few-shot CoT。
infra_impact.code: 公开 https://github.com/Spongeorge/long-context-multihop ，MIT license，含完整 prompt 模板、位置生成器、3 个数据集预处理脚本，可直接复用为 long-context LLM 的回归测试套件。

open_questions: GPT-4o / Llama-3.1-128k / DeepSeek-V3 / o1 这一代模型是否仍受 between-distance 退化影响？还是 RL 推理后训练已自然弱化此现象？
是否存在一种位置编码或 attention 变体，能在保持长上下文外推性的同时消除 between-distance 退化？
能否设计 chain-aware summarization——按 hop 关键句优先保留——从而避免裁剪带来的绝对准确率下跌？
Llama-2-longlora 在 CoT 下崩溃是真正的 primacy bias，还是 prompt template 不匹配（无 instruct format）的伪退化？需要受控 ablation。
在更长上下文（100+ docs）和更深推理链（k≥5）下，between-distance 效应是线性增长还是饱和？

papers.title: SageAttention2: Efficient Attention with Thorough Outlier Smoothing and Per-thread INT4 Quantization
papers.core_contribution: Quantizes Q,K to INT4 with per-thread granularity aligned to MMA instruction layout and P,V to FP8 with two-level accumulation, achieving 3x speedup over FlashAttention2 with negligible accuracy loss.
papers.summary: SageAttention2 introduces Smooth Q+K (subtracting token-wise means from both Q and K, lifting INT4 CosSim from 80% to 99.5%), per-thread quantization (aligning quantization groups with MMA thread layout for zero-overhead fine-grained precision), and two-level FP32 accumulation (discovering NVIDIA's FP8 MMA accumulator is actually FP22). Achieves 481 TOPS on RTX4090, 3x faster than FlashAttention2 and matches FlashAttention3(fp8) on Hopper with higher accuracy.
key_findings: Smooth Q+K raises INT4 QK^T CosSim from 80% to 99.46%
Per-thread quantization achieves per-token accuracy at zero speed overhead
NVIDIA FP8 MMA accumulator is actually FP22, not FP32 as documented
481 TOPS on RTX4090, 3x over FlashAttention2
FlashAttention3(fp8) catastrophically degrades on video generation; SageAttention2 maintains quality
limitations: INT4 tensor cores only on NVIDIA Ada; Hopper requires INT8 fallback
4-bit variant shows mild accuracy loss on some LLM tasks
Inference-only; backward pass not addressed
CUDA-only implementation

infra_impact.algorithm: Plug-and-play attention acceleration orthogonal to sparse/linear attention
infra_impact.kernel: Establishes per-thread quantization pattern aligned with hardware MMA layout
infra_impact.framework: Open-source CUDA kernels integrable into vLLM, SGLang, etc.
infra_impact.llm: Reduces first-token latency by 40-50% for 100K+ sequences
infra_impact.agent: Lower attention latency enables real-time long-context agent scenarios
infra_impact.cluster: N/A

open_questions: Can INT4/FP8 attention be extended to training backward pass?
How to port per-thread quantization to AMD MFMA layout?
How to integrate with KV cache quantization to avoid redundant quant/dequant?

papers.title: SageAttention: Accurate 8-Bit Attention for Plug-and-play Inference Acceleration
papers.core_contribution: SageAttention proposes a plug-and-play INT8 quantization method for attention that achieves 2.1x speedup over FlashAttention2 with negligible accuracy loss, by combining K-smoothing, mixed INT8/FP16 precision, and adaptive per-layer kernel selection.
papers.summary: SageAttention addresses attention quantization challenges with three key techniques: (1) smoothing K by subtracting channel-wise mean exploiting softmax shift-invariance; (2) INT8 per-block quantization for QK^T with FP16 for PV; (3) adaptive quantization selecting fastest kernel per layer. Achieves 341 TOPS on RTX4090, 2.1x faster than FlashAttention2 with negligible end-to-end metrics loss across 6 diverse models.
key_findings: K matrix channel-wise outliers eliminated via mean subtraction (softmax shift-invariance), improving worst-case CosSim from 30.6% to 99.3%
INT8 quantization more accurate than FP8 (INT8: 99.54% vs E4M3: 92.83%) and 2x faster on RTX4090
FP16 accumulator for PV matmul achieves identical accuracy to FP32 while doubling speed
Achieves 341 TOPS on RTX4090, 2.1x faster than FlashAttention2
limitations: Only evaluated on RTX4090/RTX3090; no datacenter GPU results
Only targets inference forward pass; backward pass not addressed
Adaptive quantization requires offline calibration per model

infra_impact.algorithm: Enables post-training quantized attention as generic acceleration orthogonal to weight quantization
infra_impact.kernel: INT8 attention kernel template in Triton achieving 52% of theoretical peak
infra_impact.framework: Plug-and-play sage_attn() drop-in replacement for PyTorch inference
infra_impact.llm: 2x attention speedup directly benefits prefill-bound LLM workloads
infra_impact.agent: Reduced attention latency enables faster long-context reasoning
infra_impact.cluster: N/A

open_questions: How does SageAttention perform on Hopper where FP8 TCs are faster?
Can Smooth K be extended to training backward pass?
What is the optimal mixed-precision strategy for FP4 on Blackwell?

papers.title: Make Your LLM Fully Utilize the Context
papers.core_contribution: Proposes Information-Intensive (IN2) training: a purely data-driven SFT recipe that synthesises 1.75M long-context QA examples (4K-32K tokens) where the gold information is forced to occur at uniformly-random positions, producing FilM-7B which eliminates the lost-in-the-middle problem on Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2 without any architectural change.
papers.summary: The paper hypothesises that lost-in-the-middle is caused by position bias hidden in standard pretraining and SFT data (next-token loss favours nearby tokens; system prompts always sit at position 0). To explicitly counter this bias, the authors construct an IN2 dataset by (1) splitting C4 realnewslike texts into 128-token segments, (2) prompting GPT-4-Turbo to generate either fine-grained single-segment QA (~63%) or multi-hop multi-segment QA (~17%), and (3) shuffling the gold segment(s) with random segments and using reject sampling to make both context length (4K-32K) and gold-segment position uniform. Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2 is fine-tuned for 1 epoch (14K steps, bs=128, LR 1e-6 cosine, 16x8 A100 ~300 GPU-days) on the 1.75M mixture (1.4M long QA + 150K short QA + 200K OpenOrca). The resulting FilM-7B achieves 85.9 average vs 79.0 for GPT-4-Turbo on the new VaL Probing suite (3 styles: document/code/database x 3 retrieval patterns: forward/backward/bi-directional), with the smallest min-max position gap of 13.9 (vs GPT-4 32.1). On 9 LongBench tasks the average rises from 30.6 to 39.9, and short-context performance (MMLU 59.3->59.2, etc.) is essentially preserved. An ablation shows sliding-window attention is incompatible with long-context goals, and that increasing RoPE base theta to 10x default further improves IN2 training.
key_findings: Lost-in-the-middle is a data-distribution problem, not an architecture problem - it is fixable purely via SFT data without changing attention or position encoding.
Forcing the GOLD information segment to occupy uniformly-random positions in 4K-32K contexts (via reject sampling) is the critical design choice that makes IN2 work.
Synthesised long-context QA generated from short C4 segments by GPT-4-Turbo generalises to real long-context tasks (NarrativeQA F1 23.5->26.9; LongBench avg 30.6->39.9).
FilM-7B matches or beats GPT-4-Turbo on VaL Probing average (85.9 vs 79.0) and dramatically lowers the position gap (13.9 vs 32.1), showing improved robustness rather than just higher mean.

VaL Probing (with backward and bi-directional retrieval, code and structured-data contexts) reveals that Needle-in-the-Haystack overestimates long-context capability - LongAlign and InternLM2 score near-perfect on NIH but still U-shape on VaL.
Sliding-window attention cannot coexist with long-context targets: enabling a 4K SW during IN2 training collapses retrieval accuracy beyond 4K to ~0.
Higher information intensity (not only longer context) calls for a larger RoPE base theta - 10x the default raises VaL avg by ~5 points, with diminishing returns past 1e7.
limitations: Heavy reliance on GPT-4-Turbo for QA generation makes the pipeline expensive (~$50K-$100K LLM cost) and creates an effective distillation ceiling at the teacher's quality.
Validated only at 32K context length; generalisation to 64K/128K/1M long-context settings is untested.
All synthetic data is sourced from C4 realnewslike (English news); domain coverage for code, math, dialogue, and other languages is limited.
Reject-sampled uniform length distribution diverges from real long-tailed workload distributions, with unknown effect on production behaviour.
Catastrophic forgetting is only partially mitigated - HellaSwag drops -4.5 and ARC-C drops -3.4, larger than the paper's 'minor variances' framing.
Training stability details (gradient clipping, mixed precision, loss spike handling) are not disclosed, hurting reproducibility.
No release of the full 1.75M training data; only the construction code and VaL probing data are open-sourced.

infra_impact.algorithm: Establishes a standard recipe for long-context SFT - subsequent work can adopt the IN2 idea (uniform gold-position via reject sampling + fine-grained/multi-hop QA split) as a default pattern.
infra_impact.kernel: N/A - no new kernels required; standard attention. Care needed when using fused RoPE kernels that hard-code theta=1e6 (must be retunable to 1e7).
infra_impact.framework: Mostly N/A - standard FSDP + answer-only loss. Adds a soft requirement for length-aware batch packing and reject-sampling utilities in data pipelines.
infra_impact.llm: No model architecture change. Suggests system-prompt placement should be diversified during SFT to avoid baking position bias into future LLMs.
infra_impact.agent: Direct uplift for agent runtimes that consume long tool-call histories and conversation memory - flat retrieval performance across positions improves multi-turn reliability.
infra_impact.code: Open-sourced at github.com/microsoft/FILM (data-gen prompts, VaL probing dataset, evaluation scripts); checkpoint at huggingface.co/In2Training/FILM-7B-32K. Full training data not released.

open_questions: Can a cheaper teacher (e.g. Llama-3-70B, DeepSeek-V2/V3, Qwen-2.5-72B) substitute for GPT-4-Turbo without quality loss, lowering the data-generation cost by 10x?
Does IN2 training generalise to 128K / 1M context windows, or does each new length tier require fresh data with appropriately resized segments?
What is the optimal multi-hop ratio (paper uses 17%); does pushing it to 50% trade single-segment retrieval for stronger reasoning?
Can IN2-style position-uniform synthetic data be added to PRETRAINING (not just SFT), bypassing the catastrophic forgetting risk seen on HellaSwag/ARC-C?
How does FilM-7B perform on multilingual long-context tasks given that all training data is English C4?

papers.title: NVIDIA Blackwell Architecture Technical Brief
papers.core_contribution: 双Die统一GPU突破光刻极限(NV-HBI 10TB/s)，FP4 Tensor Core + 第二代Transformer Engine，NVLink5(1.8TB/s) + NVL72机架级GPU域
papers.summary: Blackwell架构采用TSMC 4NP工艺，208B晶体管，由两个reticle-limited die通过NV-HBI(10TB/s)连接为统一GPU。核心创新：(1)FP4 Tensor Core + 微缩放格式，估计FP4峰值~10 PFLOPS；(2)第二代Transformer Engine支持FP4/FP8/FP16动态精度；(3)NVLink 5每GPU 1.8TB/s，14x PCIe Gen5；(4)NVL72: 72-GPU机架级NVLink域130TB/s；(5)解压缩引擎800GB/s；(6)机密计算TEE扩展到GPU。GB200 Superchip集成2个Blackwell GPU + 1个Grace CPU(NVLink-C2C 900GB/s)。192GB HBM3e, 8TB/s带宽。

208B晶体管, TSMC 4NP
FP4 Tensor Core + 微缩放格式支持

papers.summary: AMD CDNA架构是AMD首次将GPU架构分化为计算专用(CDNA)和图形专用(RDNA)的产物。CDNA移除了所有图形固定功能单元，引入Matrix Core Engine支持MFMA指令族，支持FP32/FP16/BF16/INT8矩阵运算。首款产品MI100在7nm工艺下实现120个CU，首次突破10 TFLOPS FP64大关(11.5 TFLOPS)。配备32GB HBM2、1.23TB/s带宽，3条Infinity Fabric链路实现4-GPU全互联。ROCm 4.0提供完整Exascale软件栈。
key_findings: 首次将GPU分化为CDNA(计算)和RDNA(图形)两个独立架构
Matrix Core Engine引入MFMA指令族，FP16矩阵性能比MI50提升7x
120 CU, 11.5 TFLOPS FP64，首次突破10TF FP64大关
32GB HBM2, 1.23TB/s带宽, 8MB L2
3条Infinity Fabric链路实现4-GPU全互联拓扑
limitations: 无FP8支持
无结构化稀疏
显存仅32GB
BF16 Matrix性能仅FP16的一半

infra_impact.algorithm: BF16支持降低ML训练精度门槛
infra_impact.kernel: MFMA指令族需要全新kernel开发
infra_impact.framework: ROCm 4.0 + HIP提供CUDA移植路径
infra_impact.llm: N/A - LLM时代尚未到来
infra_impact.agent: N/A

papers.title: NVIDIA A100 Tensor Core GPU Architecture White Paper
papers.core_contribution: 第三代Tensor Core支持全数据类型(含FP64 TC和TF32)，引入MIG多实例GPU和2:4结构化稀疏，奠定数据中心GPU标准
papers.summary: A100基于7nm GA100 GPU(826mm², 54.2B晶体管)，108个SM，432个第三代Tensor Core。核心创新：(1)TF32格式让FP32输入无修改获10x加速；(2)FP64 Tensor Core首次使HPC利用TC加速(19.5 TFLOPS)；(3)2:4结构化稀疏TC吞吐翻倍；(4)MIG将单GPU分为7个独立实例。80GB HBM2e, 2TB/s带宽, 40MB L2。NVLink 3.0提供600GB/s。400W TDP。
key_findings: 108 SM, 432 Tensor Cores (3rd gen)
TF32: 156 TFLOPS (312 sparse) — FP32无修改即可加速
FP64 TC: 19.5 TFLOPS — HPC首次可用TC
2:4结构化稀疏: TC吞吐翻倍
MIG: 单GPU分为最多7个实例
80GB HBM2e, 2TB/s, 40MB L2
NVLink 3: 12 links, 600GB/s
limitations: 无FP8支持
80GB显存对LLM渐显不足
NVLink域限于单节点8 GPU

infra_impact.algorithm: TF32使混合精度训练零成本入门
infra_impact.kernel: Async copy + L2 residency control改变kernel优化策略
infra_impact.framework: MIG改变云端GPU资源调度模型
infra_impact.llm: 80GB限制大模型单卡推理
infra_impact.agent: MIG适合多租户inference serving
